**Taking the course?** Go to **[START HERE.md](START%20HERE.md)** (or `index.html` once built). This README is for operators and contributors.

# Expand the Box v2 — operator and contributor orientation

This folder is **v2** of the digital Expand the Box course: the same course taught better. v1 proved the teaching content, the safety architecture, and the tool engineering. v2 adds what v1 lacked: a real front door and HTML shell, one evolving daily practice wired into every module, a retrieval layer inside the no-quiz stance, the canon and safety patches, a sanctioned solo path, and the operator/commerce layer that lets a real person launch a cohort.

The build plan is locked in [`_build/ARCHITECTURE.md`](_build/ARCHITECTURE.md) (§1's eight decisions are the law of this build). The audit it came from is [`_build/AUDIT.md`](_build/AUDIT.md).

---

## Folder map

| Folder / file | What lives there |
|---|---|
| `START HERE.md` | THE learner front door (un-prefixed so it sorts first) |
| `index.html` | The same front door as HTML, home of the static shell (built in W-B4) |
| `00 - Course Manifest.md` | What-lives-where registry: module table, map registry, localStorage keys, link conventions |
| `01 - Course Architecture and Pedagogy.md` | Why the course is shaped this way; the retrieval line; shipped-format specs |
| `02 - Async Delivery Framework.md` | How delivery works: artifacts, reference platform stack, solo mode |
| `03 - Safety and Facilitation Framework.md` | The safety spine: agreements, grounding, consent spec, exit ramps |
| `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` | **The law.** Intake screens, gates, witness protocol, cohort cap |
| `05 - Operator Run-Book.md` | **The route.** Launch sequence Step 0–5 with done-when criteria |
| `Days/` | The twelve module files, Day 00 through Day 11 (markdown sources) |
| `Course/` | Rendered HTML module pages (generated by `_build/build.py`, W-B4) |
| `Practice/` | Daily Practice Spine · Beep! Book Guide · Feelings Form · Coming Back · My Map Book |
| `Maps/` | Map images: M01–M29 from v1 plus the eleven new clean renders (see Manifest) |
| `Map Notes/` | One-page map cards: embedded map, summary, links to module and Atlas tool |
| `Map Atlas/` | Interactive map-study pages, one per shipped map ("Study the map") |
| `Interactive Tools/` | Per-module practice tools ("Run the practice") plus `journal.html` and `ground.html` |
| `Facilitator Resources/` | CM and operator pack: playbooks, templates, banks, the Voice Guide, the Video Manifest |
| `_assets/` | Self-hosted fonts (no CDN calls) |
| `_build/` | ARCHITECTURE, AUDIT, build pipeline, link-check reports. Never learner-facing |
| `LICENSE.md` | CC BY-SA 4.0 terms and the honest caveats |

## Doc hierarchy

**Doc 04 is the law, the Run-Book is the route.** `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` binds every cohort: screens, thresholds, gates, the cap. `05 - Operator Run-Book.md` sequences it for an operator; `Facilitator Resources/CM First-Cohort Run-Book.md` sequences it for the CM. Where any other file disagrees with Doc 04, Doc 04 wins. Where wording disagrees with the canonical-phrasing ledger in the Voice Guide, the ledger wins.

## Relationship to v1

v1 lives at [`../Digital Expand The Box May 2026/`](../Digital%20Expand%20The%20Box%20May%202026/) and is the **frozen template**: read-only, never modified, the reference for every rewrite. Copied folders keep v1 names so relative links survive. The four confirmed v1 canon bugs (high drama, bright/shadow causation, matrix definition, EHP position count) are corrected in v2 per the ledger; do not copy v1 wording for those distinctions.

## Contribution rules

1. **The [Voice and Style Guide](Facilitator%20Resources/Voice%20and%20Style%20Guide.md) is binding** for every file, human- or AI-written. Read it before drafting; run its pre-flight checklist before submitting.
2. **The canonical-phrasing ledger** (Voice Guide §6) controls every PM distinction. State distinctions in ledger-compatible words. The three flagged CORRECTIONS override all v1 text.
3. **No gamification, no quizzes.** No badges, streaks, scores, certificates, answer-checking, or right/wrong feedback, anywhere. The retrieval line in Doc 01 defines what recall practice is allowed (generation yes, grading no).
4. **Link convention:** relative paths, spaces percent-encoded (`Days/Day%2001%20-%20...`). Learner-facing files link only paths that ship in this folder. The link checker is a release gate.
5. **Consent is never persisted.** Every gated tool re-arms its consent block on every visit. No `etb_*consent*` key may ever be written to localStorage. This is a safety invariant, not a style choice.
6. Learner-facing markdown ends with the World Copyleft footer block, exactly as the Voice Guide §8 gives it.

1. Relative paths only; spaces percent-encoded (`%20`) in every link target.
2. Learner-facing files link only paths that ship in this folder. No `Possability Lab`/`SPARKS` source-library paths, no v1 paths, no bare `.mp4` filenames.

---

# 01 — Course Architecture and Pedagogy

## What this course is

**Expand the Box** is a twelve-module (00–11), self-paced online course that teaches the core thoughtware of **Possibility Management (PM)**: the body of distinctions, maps and practices developed by Clinton Callahan and the PM community since the late 1980s.

It is a **thoughtware upgrade**. The learner's Box, the survival-and-protection thoughtware they have been identified with, becomes visible enough that they can disidentify from it, enter liquid state, choose a new context, and run experiments from there. The course is not "self-improvement"; it is an apprenticeship in catching the Box in the act and choosing differently while it is happening.

It is **not** therapy, coaching, or a workshop of prepackaged techniques. It is an apprenticeship in noticing, supported by maps, distinctions, embodied practices, partner pairings, one evolving daily practice, and structured between-module experiments. v2's framing claim: this is a **practice container, not a content library**. Every architectural choice below serves that.

---

## The pedagogical premise

PM rests on a single load-bearing claim:

> What you are able to think with determines what becomes possible to choose, feel, speak and create. Upgrading thoughtware is therefore upstream of any change in behavior, relationship, or what life can become.

Everything follows from that:

1. **Maps before practice.** Each module installs the relevant map first, then asks the learner to live inside it. A learner who tries to "do anger differently" without a new map of feelings reproduces the old map's habits in different costume.
2. **Distinctions before techniques.** PM gives **distinctions** the learner uses, not techniques to master. A distinction is a perceptual cut; once seen, it changes what is available. Examples: *feeling vs emotion*, *Box vs you*, *low drama vs responsible game*, *is-glue vs has-glue*.
3. **Five-body learning.** Cognitive content alone reinstalls the Box. Each module engages at least three of the five bodies (intellectual, emotional, physical, energetic, archetypal) so the learning lands where new behavior originates.
4. **Practice in the small.** New choices stabilize through small experiments: a single sentence said in a single conversation. Not a transformation. A rep. The reps compound.
5. **No authority transfer.** Prompts ask the learner to verify each distinction in their own direct experience, not to take the writer's word.

---

## Key distinctions baked into the architecture

Seven distinctions sit underneath every module. They carry forward from v1 unchanged in substance; canonical wordings live in the [Voice and Style Guide](Facilitator%20Resources/Voice%20and%20Style%20Guide.md) ledger, which wins any wording dispute.

1. **Context (the PM definition).** The invisible source-frame that determines what actions, meanings, choices, and results are even available. Upstream of mindset and beliefs. The course works on context; most learners arrive trying to change behavior.
2. **Possibility paradigm vs causal paradigm.** The causal paradigm explains the present from the past; the possibility paradigm creates from a chosen context. The course does not ask the learner to abandon causal explanation, only to spend twelve modules testing context as the more leveraged frame.
3. **Gameworld.** The course is a gameworld the learner enters by choice: explicit rules (the four agreements), roles (learner · partner/witness · CM · CM's supervisor), agreements, consequences, a duration, and a boundary. Naming it tells the learner they are entering an explicit, finite container, not joining anything.
4. **Archiarchy vs patriarchy.** PM points toward archiarchy: culture generated by sourcing bright principles. Patriarchy, the cultural matrix most learners were raised inside, is generated by sourcing shadow principles. (Causation direction per ledger CORRECTION 2: principle upstream, culture downstream.) The course asks for experiments, not identity adoption.
5. **Box layers.** Surface (behaviors), story (beliefs and narratives), survival (identity-level commitments formed under threat). The course paces these: Module 2 stays mostly at surface and story; Modules 5–6 touch survival material through the emotional body; Module 9 gives the tools to work it more safely.
6. **Awareness vs consciousness.** Awareness notices; consciousness holds distinctions and chooses from them. PM is a consciousness training. Mindfulness builds awareness; PM uses awareness to install distinctions, then uses distinctions to enable choice.
7. **Being vs Box vs Gremlin.** Three locations to speak, feel, and choose from. The learner has a Box and has a Gremlin and is neither. Module 9's five ego states only work if this distinction is already held.

One more architectural term, fixed by ledger CORRECTION 3: **matrix** means, everywhere by default, *the energetic structure you build through practice that determines how much consciousness you can hold*. Thoughtware upgrades install onto matrix. The cultural-matrix sense is always written with a qualifier; the old "structured field" delivery sense is retired in favor of "container."

---

## The learning arc (why this order)

The arc moves **container → frame → embodiment → emotional body → relational body → integration → continuation**, each module installing what the next assumes.

| Phase | Modules | What this phase installs |
|---|---|---|
| **Container** | 00 | The container itself: cohort, recruited witness, or solo path · Beep! Book · the schedule |
| **Frame** | 1, 2 | New context · the chain · radical responsibility · the Box as filter |
| **Embodiment infrastructure** | 3, 4 | Liquid state · center/ground/bubble · five bodies · feedback · experiments |
| **Emotional body** | 5, 6 | Feelings vs emotions · old map · Numbness Bar · mixed emotions · EHP |
| **Relational body** | 7, 8 | Low drama and gremlin food · the Responsible Game · listening · speaking · completion loops |
| **Integration** | 9, 10 | Ego states · problem ownership · learning spiral · Map of Possibility · bright/shadow principles · three powers |
| **Continuation** | 11 | The 90-day container · Possibility Team · stopping well · what the learner takes with them |

The v1 rationale holds: container before map (without the authored stance, maps become information to consume); embodiment before feelings (Module 3 installs the equipment Modules 5–6 use); emotional before relational (low drama is mechanically an avoidance of one's own feelings); ego states near the end (the integration lens).

**The climb is named.** Modules 5–9 stack four High modules and a heavy Medium. The architecture cushions it deliberately: the integration pause after Module 6 (at least one full low-demand day, with a 15-minute recall-based consolidation rep inside it), the corrected Module 7 framing (Modules 8 and 9 are still ahead), and a scheduled rest day between Modules 8 and 9. Module 00's printable schedule shows all of this before the learner starts.

---

## THE RETRIEVAL LINE (binding; contributors do not re-litigate this)

The course's no-quiz stance (Doc 02 §J) is load-bearing: PM is verified in lived experience, and the course rewards nothing. v2 adds a retrieval layer **inside** that stance. The line between the two is drawn here, once, for good:

**Generation: YES.** Any practice where the learner produces the material from their own memory and experience, and the course's only response is the learner's own next look at the map:

- **Free recall** — every module opens with a 5-minute warm-up: redraw or restate the prior module's central map from memory, then look at the map.
- **Teach-back** — the learner explains a map to their partner in their own words (Modules 3, 6, 9), seeded by the Map Notes' "How to explain it verbally" paragraphs.
- **Self-explanation** — "say why this step is here before reading on."
- **Perform-the-outcome** — show the move (run a completion loop, do a bar reading) rather than describe knowing it.
- **Unscored flip-card recall** — the Atlas flip-cards, framed as recall prompts: see the term, say the distinction out loud, flip. Nothing records whether the learner "got it."
- **Close-the-loop self-check** — each module ends with the existing three-word scale (the Learning Self-Assessment's *not yet / partly / landed*), self-applied, never collected per-name.

**Grading: NO. Forever.** Anything where the course (a tool, a page, a person in a course role) checks an answer and renders a verdict:

- Answer-checking of any kind. No tool compares the learner's recall to a stored correct answer.
- Scoring, points, percentages, streaks, progress bars framed as achievement.
- Right/wrong feedback, including softened forms ("almost!", "correct!").

The test for any proposed feature: **does the learner generate, and does the learner alone judge?** Both yes: inside the line. Either no: outside, rejected. The map itself is the answer key, and the learner's own comparison is the feedback channel. A teach-back partner witnesses and reflects what they heard; they do not mark it.

This resolves AUDIT open question 7. Cite this section instead of reopening it.

---

## The daily practice spine

The course has exactly **one** daily practice: a 5–10 minute morning sit that starts as a Beep! Book capture (Phase A, Modules 00–04) and folds a Feelings Form bar reading into the same sit from Module 5 (Phase B). Module 8 adds one line item to the Form, not a practice. One anchor, evolving content, honoring the Experiment Bank's one-at-a-time bandwidth rule.

**[Practice/Daily Practice Spine.md](Practice/Daily%20Practice%20Spine.md) is the binding spec**: the exact sit steps, the script wiring (Scripts 2/3/5/9 at fixed flow points), the canonical header lines every module pastes, and the rule that no module may invent a second daily practice. Module writers amend that file or they do not touch the spine.

---

## Sittings architecture

Every module runs in **2–3 sittings** (two for Low, three for High and the heavier Mediums). The files stay whole; the sittings are marked, not split. This is the cognitive-load fix: a learner who reads a High module in one push arrives at the embodied practice depleted.

The break block is uniform across all modules. Format (the spec; module writers paste and fill):

```
---

**End of Sitting 1 (of 3).** Stop here. Stand, water, daylight if you can get it.
Come back after a real break — an hour minimum, a day is fine.
Sitting 2 opens with: [name of the next section].

---
```

Three rules: the marker names which sitting just ended and how many there are; it names what the next sitting opens with (so re-entry needs no re-reading); and the module's flow table shows the same sitting boundaries. Practices are never split across a sitting boundary mid-practice.

---

## Teach-backs and the feedback repeat (why these exist)

Doc 01's v1 edition named the fatal failure mode: **intellectual swallowing**. A learner consumes the distinctions, nods, and installs nothing; procedure without verbal command of the map degrades within weeks, and the High block sits right after the point where v1's practice-quality feedback ended (Module 4).

v2's countermeasures, scheduled, not optional:

- **Teach-back #1, Module 3** · **#2, Module 6** · **#3, Module 9.** The learner teaches one named map to their partner in a voice message, in their own words, before the module's exchange. The partner reflects what they heard land and what stayed fog. Framed as verified-in-lived-experience, never as a test. Source material: the relevant Map Note's "How to explain it verbally" paragraph, read *after* the attempt, not before.
- **The Module 8 feedback-quality repeat.** Module 4's feedback round runs again at Module 8 on the learner's current material. By Module 8 the exchanges have been witnessing-only for four modules; the repeat re-calibrates the practice quality right where the course's hardest relational work happens.

---

## How distinctions cascade (prerequisite chains, kept from v1)

- *Box vs you* (Module 2) becomes operational only after *new context* (Module 1). Without a new context, "I have a Box" is self-criticism.
- *Liquid state* (Module 3) requires a visible Box to loosen.
- *Feeling vs emotion* (Module 5) requires the five bodies (Module 3), specifically emotional body vs intellectual body.
- *Low drama* (Module 7) requires the new map of feelings (Module 5); drama is structurally an avoidance of clean feeling.
- *Three powers* (Module 10) requires *radical responsibility* (Module 1) and *bright principles* (Module 10). Without those, "I declare" is grandiosity.

The course resists skipping ahead. The **readiness checks** enforce it where it matters: 10-second self-checks before each High module (in the [Learning Self-Assessment](Facilitator%20Resources/Learning%20Self-Assessment.md), Part 2) plus the structural unlock checklist in Doc 04 §D. A learner who fails a readiness self-check re-runs the named prior practice; that is the check working.

---

## Shipped-format specs (what each artifact actually is)

The single source for *which* artifacts each module has is the [Course Manifest](00%20-%20Course%20Manifest.md) §1 table. This section specs the formats; it replaces v1's Map Note template section, which described a format that no longer shipped.

**Module file** (in `Days/`), in order:

1. Header block: title · purpose · intensity tag · **daily-spine phase line** (verbatim from the Spine spec) · honest time estimate · sittings count · core PM concepts · learning outcomes. High modules add: consent block pointer, GROUND NOW pointer, re-entry link ([Practice/Coming Back](Practice/Coming%20Back.md)).
2. **Free-recall warm-up** (5 min, prior module's central map).
3. Maps introduced (each with embed where shipped, "Study the map" Atlas link, Map Note link).
4. Module flow table with sitting boundaries and "Run the practice" tool links at the moment of use.
5. Concept teaching notes (with "Common misunderstandings" refutation blocks after every map, and "what to expect" calibration plus named drift signals on every micro-practice).
6. Embodied practice (solo) · 7. Partner exchange (async) · 8. Between-module experiment (Day-4 format: *what / by when / what I will notice*, one at a time, plus one callback rep to a prior instrument) · 9. Reflection prompts · 10. Safety callouts (module-specific predicted failure modes; Screen-4 variant cue where flagged) · 11. **Close-the-loop self-check** (three-word scale) · 12. Cohort feed post · 13. Glossary additions · 14. World Copyleft footer.

Solo-reader boxes appear where the Manifest flags them (Modules 00, 5, 6, 7, 9). A videos-are-enrichment note replaces every v1 video reference, pointing to the [Video Manifest](Facilitator%20Resources/Video%20Manifest.md).

**Map Note card** (in `Map Notes/`): map name + one-sentence framing · embedded map image (or text-first note) · **What it is** paragraph · **At a glance** compressed distinctions · the card block linking full teaching (module) and interactive tool (Atlas page). Cards are the distinction-density layer and the teach-back source; they stay terse.

**Atlas page** (in `Map Atlas/`): one map, self-contained HTML on the shared design system; the map walked through interactively; unscored flip-cards framed as recall prompts; GROUND NOW in the header; completion dot + reflections in `etb_atlas_M##` localStorage; links back to module and card. Atlas M12 carries no live EHP: it describes and routes to the gated Day 6 walker.

**Interactive tool** (in `Interactive Tools/Day NN/`): the module's "Run the practice" surface. Conventions: shared design system; per-visit consent component on gated tools (never persisted, day-specific items, `inert` gating, GROUND NOW reachable from the gate); localStorage only, keys registered in the Manifest §6; escapeHtml on all user input; real keyboard a11y; self-hosted fonts, zero external calls.

---

## Voice

The full voice spec lives in the **[Voice and Style Guide](Facilitator%20Resources/Voice%20and%20Style%20Guide.md)** and is binding for every contributor. In one paragraph: direct, distinction-rich, occasionally blunt, closer to Callahan than to corporate L&D; PM vocabulary unsoftened; every paragraph survives the cut test; concrete verbs; no motivational filler; sources cited where a learner might verify; safety always names a trigger signal, a physical action, and a next decision. The guide adds the enforcement layer: em-dash budget, signature-word caps, banned patterns, the canonical-phrasing ledger, and the verbatim voice-anchor scripts. Run its pre-flight checklist before submitting any file.

---

## Versioning and revision

This is **course architecture v2**. The locked formats (module shape, sittings block, spine header lines) and the four working agreements cannot change mid-cohort, only between full runs. Teaching content revises continuously as post-cohort reviews (Doc 04 §L) surface patterns. The retrieval line above does not version: it is settled.

---

<!-- ===== source: 02 - Async Delivery Framework.md ===== -->

---

# 02 — Async Delivery Framework

This document is the **operational manual** for delivering Expand the Box as a self-paced asynchronous online course. A community manager, course operator, or solo cohort steward reads it in full before opening a cohort. The launch sequence that uses it lives in [05 - Operator Run-Book.md](05%20-%20Operator%20Run-Book.md); the binding safety protocol it serves is [04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md](04%20-%20Container%20and%20Gatekeeping%20Protocol.md).

The course works without a live facilitator on every module. It does **not** work without structure. The structure is here.

---

## A. The learner's module cycle

A learner moves through one module per **~3 days**. The rhythm per module:

| Cycle day | Action | Time |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Read the module (Sitting 1, then 2–3 with real breaks) + solo embodied practice | 60–120 min total across sittings |
| 1 or 2 | Record partner check-in (voice message) | 5–10 min |
| 2–3 | Run the between-module experiment in real life | no scheduled time; it rides ordinary life |
| 3 | Listen to partner's reply; record reflection back; journal; one line to the cohort feed | 25–30 min |
| every day | The daily spine sit (Phase A or B per module header) | 5–10 min |

Total active time: ~2 hours on Low modules, ~3–3.5 on High (5, 6, 7, 9); each module header carries its own honest estimate, which is authoritative. Async partner-reply latency (24h SLA) can stretch a High module's calendar time, especially Module 6.

Twelve modules at ~3 days ≈ **~35 days** end to end, including the integration pause after Module 6 and the rest day between Modules 8 and 9. Compressed (~25 days) and stretched (~60–70) both work as long as the rhythm of *recall → content → practice → experiment → reflection* and the two pause beats are preserved. The pause beats are not compressible.

---

## B. Module artifacts (what the learner actually receives)

Each module delivers, in order:

1. **The written module file** (`Days/Day NN - ….md`, rendered as `Course/module-NN.html` in the shell). The single canonical source for the module. **Every module is complete without video** (see §E below).
2. **The daily-spine instruction** in the header: which phase the morning sit is in, linked to the binding spec ([Practice/Daily Practice Spine.md](Practice/Daily%20Practice%20Spine.md)).
3. **THE TOOL SLOT.** Every module names its two interactive surfaces at the moment of use, as buttons in the flow:
   - **"Study the map"** → the module's Map Atlas pages (interactive walk-through of each map taught).
   - **"Run the practice"** → the module's Interactive Tools suite (the practice instrument for that module).
   - Standing utilities, every module: **[the journal](Interactive%20Tools/journal.html)** (local-only progress view, export/import of all `etb_*` records) and **[ground.html](Interactive%20Tools/ground.html)** (the 60-second grounding script, bookmarkable, one tap).
   - The Manifest §1 table registers which pages and tools belong to which module. Learners never see "two systems"; they see two buttons.
4. **Map Note cards** for the maps introduced.
5. **The partner prompt** (inside the module file's exchange section).
6. **The experiment card** (inside the module file, Day-4 format).
7. **Reflection prompts.**

Everything ships as text/HTML from one folder. Nothing requires an account, a server round-trip, or a video player.

---

## C. Pairing partners

### Pairing as the witness strand of the container

The course's container is woven from several strands: the learner's declared context (the red-pill commitment), the four working agreements, the body practices, the daily spine, the cohort boundary, the CM's gatekeeping, and the **partner pairing**. (v1 called this structure "the training matrix"; v2 reserves *matrix* for the build sense per the ledger and calls this the **container**.)

The pairing is the witness strand: a structured place to speak the learner's material out loud, a witness who listens without advising, a presence that interrupts isolation on the High modules. What the partner does **not** hold: the learner's safety container, clinical care, commitment, or responsibility for their own state. The partner agreement (Doc 04 §B) is signed before any exchange; a learner without a live pairing (or recruited witness, §K below) cannot unlock High modules.

### C.1 Matching rules

- **Match before Module 1.** Late enrollers are matched before content access opens.
- **Match on cadence first, content preference second.** Compatible daily windows beat philosophical fit.
- **Avoid pre-existing close ties** for cohort pairing. If unavoidable in a small cohort, name the dynamic and add a witness partner. (The recruit-a-witness path for solo readers deliberately uses a friend; the difference is scope: a recruited witness holds the High-module witness role only, not the full course-long exchange. Doc 04 §B2.)
- **Re-match by Module 4 if it is not working.** Silent partnerships are worse than swapped ones.

### C.2 Voice-message exchanges (default mode)

Voice carries tone, breath, and the texture of the emotional body; text strips it. The platform must meet the privacy minimums in Doc 04 §H: private 1:1 thread, no automatic AI transcription, no public forwarding, deletion option, end-to-end encryption preferred (Signal is the reference pick). Public servers, group chats, and auto-summarizing platforms do not qualify.

Default shape per module: A records 3–8 min on the partner prompt → B replies within 24 hours, 3–8 min: *what I heard · what I noticed in myself · one question I'm leaving with you*. Partners do not advise, fix, diagnose, or pretend to feel what they do not feel. Module 8 teaches the listening formally; until then the "we're learning this" frame covers clumsy exchanges.

### C.3 Scheduled async calls (alternative)

A 30-minute scheduled call substitutes: ~10 minutes each, uninterrupted, the other witnessing.

---

## D. The cohort feed

One shared private space; learners post a line or two after each module: what landed, what is sticking sideways, optionally a question or a witness offer. Not posted: long confessionals (the partner exchange holds those), other people's stories, advice (CM redirects, verbatim language in [Cohort Feed Templates](Facilitator%20Resources/Cohort%20Feed%20Templates.md) §4).

Moderation: CM reads every post within 48 hours (24 during High windows), triages each against Doc 04 §F (yellow = DM within 24h; red = immediate, crisis numbers in the first message), and keeps the feed narrow.

---

## E. Video posture (text-first, declared)

**Every module is complete without video.** The written module is the canonical teaching; source videos are optional enrichment. The operator-facing registry of what exists and where to get it is [`Facilitator Resources/Video Manifest.md`](Facilitator%20Resources/Video%20Manifest.md). Binding rule: **no bare video filename appears anywhere in the learner path**; learner-facing text says "optional enrichment: see the Video Manifest" and nothing more specific. The [Audio Companion Recording Kit](Facilitator%20Resources/Audio%20Companion%20Recording%20Kit.md) gives an operator the scripts and tone notes to record guided audio for every practice script in an afternoon; the course ships scripts, not recordings.

---

## F. REFERENCE PLATFORM STACK (the named pick)

v1 offered a platform menu with no pick; that gap is closed. Per ARCHITECTURE decision 2, the reference stack is:

**1. The self-hosted static shell — one origin.** `index.html` (the front door) + `Course/module-NN.html` pages + the Map Atlas + the Interactive Tools, all served from **one origin**. Any static host works; **GitHub Pages works** since the repo is public; even `file://` works for a single learner. One origin is not cosmetic: the tools' localStorage records and the journal's export view are origin-scoped, so splitting the tools across hosts silently splits the learner's records.

**2. The cohort feed — a private space off the shell.** Reference pick: a **Discourse private category** (closed to the cohort, author-deletable posts, CM can hide, not externally visible). Lighter alternative: a **Signal group** (E2EE, but no post-hiding and weaker triage ergonomics; acceptable for cohorts ≤8). Either must pass Doc 04 §H before launch.

**3. Partner channel — partners choose** from the §H-compliant list (Signal reference pick).

**4. Gating — manual, per the Run-Book.** The static shell has no server logic, so High-module gating is procedural: the CM verifies the Doc 04 §D unlock checklist per learner and releases the module link (the Run-Book sequences this). The consent components inside the gated tools enforce the per-visit consent layer regardless.

### Per-component privacy table

| Component | Where data lives | What the operator can see | External calls |
|---|---|---|---|
| Static shell (pages, Atlas, tools) | learner's browser only; all `etb_*` keys are **localStorage, never transmitted** | nothing | **zero** — fonts self-hosted in `_assets/fonts/`, no CDN, no analytics, no trackers |
| `journal.html` export | a file the learner saves locally | nothing | zero |
| Cohort feed (Discourse private category) | the Discourse instance | posts (metadata-only triage log) | per instance; self-host or EU host for GDPR posture |
| Partner channel (Signal) | the partners' devices, E2EE | nothing (tracker logs *that* exchanges happened, never content) | Signal infrastructure |
| 1:1 CM channel | per chosen tool (Signal reference) | message content, logged metadata-only | per tool |
| Consent (all gated tools) | **nowhere — per-visit, never persisted** | nothing | zero |

Verification before launch is Step 1 of the [Operator Run-Book](05%20-%20Operator%20Run-Book.md). The v1 platform menu survives as Appendix A, marked *alternatives*.

---

## G. SOLO MODE (sanctioned, gates intact)

The license guarantees readers without a cohort; the course holds a real path for them instead of pretending they do not exist. Per ARCHITECTURE decision 3:

**What solo readers can do, fully:** all Low and Medium modules; the daily spine; the Atlas and the tools; the journal; the experiments (Screen-4 self-check applies, see Doc 03); the self-assessment.

**What stays gated, and the two named paths through the gate.** The High modules (5, 6, 7, 9) keep the witness requirement. The structural partner gate is preserved; what broadens is the path to satisfy it:

- **(a) Recruit-a-witness.** A friend qualifies. The instrument is the 7-line agreement; the protocol (who qualifies, what the witness reads, what disqualifies) is Doc 04 §B2. With a recruited witness, the High modules open as designed.
- **(b) Defer-list.** Without a witness, the solo reader reads the High teaching in full, runs the locator-level practices (bar readings, drama detection, ego-state location), and **defers the witnessed practices** (the EHP and Demon-adjacent work) until a witness exists. The module files mark exactly which practices defer.

**Why the gate holds even solo:** the EHP's witness is not ceremony; it is the containment mechanism for material that can exceed what a person alone can re-ground from. A course that waives that for convenience is not safer for being more accessible.

"Reading this without a cohort?" boxes appear in `START HERE.md` and in the Module 5/6/7/9 consent blocks, with [findahelpline.com](https://findahelpline.com) named as the no-cohort referral floor. Solo readers have no CM and no SLA; the boxes say so plainly.

---

## H. Optional live integration calls

Two optional live calls per cohort, unchanged from v1: **mid-course after Module 6** (placed after the heaviest module, before Module 7; integration and a breath, not a warm-up; 15 minutes may run as opt-in feedback calibration per Doc 01) and **end-of-course after Module 11** (declarations round, gratitude, what's-next). Recording rules per Doc 04 §H: personal shares never recorded; teaching segments only with per-call consent from everyone.

### The pause architecture (built into the cadence, not optional)

- **After Module 6:** at least one full low-demand day. No new content; ground, sleep, journal, partner check-in, and the 15-minute recall consolidation rep. CMs hold this even on compressed cadence.
- **Between Modules 8 and 9:** one scheduled rest day. Module 8 is as demanding as the High modules for many learners; Module 9 opens ego-state material. The rest day is on the printable schedule from Module 00.

---

## I. The community manager role

The CM is the **container caretaker**, not the teacher. Responsibilities (pre-cohort matching and packets, per-module feed triage and tracker checks, High-week vigilance, closure, the referral list, never therapy) are unchanged from v1 and sequenced in the two run-books. The binding qualification bar is Doc 04 §J; the **binding first-cohort cap (8–12 learners) and the hours model** live in Doc 04 §K2 and the [Operator Run-Book](05%20-%20Operator%20Run-Book.md) Step 2. A CM holding more than the cap is the likeliest safety failure in the whole design; the cap is part of the launch gate.

---

## J. The referral pathway

Every learner receives a per-learner referral packet generated from intake (country, region, language): crisis line first, licensed-clinician directory, PM-aware coaches, DV/legal resources if Screen 4 returned yes, the not-therapy disclaimer. Generated and acknowledged before content access opens; the CM verifies. Template: [Facilitator Resources/Referral List.md](Facilitator%20Resources/Referral%20List.md). France's national line is **3114** (24/7).

---

## K. Cadence variants

| Variant | Pace | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Default** | ~3 days/module, ~35 days | Best for most learners |
| **Compressed** | ~2 days/module, ~25 days | Only with real bandwidth; the two pause beats still apply |
| **Stretched** | 4–6 days/module, 40–70 days | Default offer for screen-flagged and bereavement-flagged learners |
| **Pulsed** | modules in bursts with week-long breaks | CM keeps pairings alive at re-entry |

Pace is the learner's; the CM aligns the partner. Mismatch protocol: Doc 04 §I.

---

## L. What this framework deliberately leaves out

- **Gamification, badges, streaks, completion certificates.** They feed the Gremlin's appetite for approval and completion points. PM does not reward compliance.
- **Quizzes and answer-checking.** PM is verified in lived experience. The retrieval layer (free recall, teach-backs, flip-card recall) lives entirely on the generation side of **the retrieval line in Doc 01**, which defines exactly what is allowed and what never will be. Contributors read that section before proposing anything recall-shaped.
- **Discussion forums for "questions about PM."** The feed stays narrow.
- **An app, accounts, analytics.** A folder of pages is enough, and the privacy table in §F is only honest because of it.

---

## Appendix A — platform menu (alternatives, not the pick)

The v1 menu, kept for operators with existing infrastructure. Any combination of (Teachable / Podia / Kajabi / Notion / Circle) for content + (Telegram / WhatsApp E2EE / Voxer / Signal) for partner voice + (closed forum / moderated shared doc) for the feed can work **if** every component passes Doc 04 §H and the tools still serve from one origin. Commercial platforms that re-host the HTML tools on their own domains split localStorage from the Atlas; test the journal before committing. The reference stack in §F exists because every one of these alternatives has failed at least one of those checks somewhere.

---

<!-- ===== source: 03 - Safety and Facilitation Framework.md ===== -->

---

# 03 — Safety and Facilitation Framework

> This document is the operational safety baseline for Expand the Box delivered async online.
> A community manager (CM) reads it cover-to-cover before opening a cohort.
> A learner reads sections **B**, **C**, **D**, **F** and **I** before starting Module 1 (Module 00 assembles them in learner voice).
> Section letters A–M match v1 so existing cross-references hold; v2 additions are sections N–Q and the marked upgrades inside A, H and I.

---

## A. What this course is not

**Expand the Box is not:**
- Therapy
- Counseling
- Trauma treatment
- Medical or psychiatric care
- A substitute for any of the above

It is an educational course in the thoughtware of Possibility Management. It will bring into view: Box survival strategies the learner has been identified with, feelings that have been unfelt, numbness in places that used to be alive, Low Drama payoffs the learner has been collecting, old decisions made under threat that are still running. That is by design. Discomfort is part of the work. Discomfort that overwhelms the learner is **not** part of the work and is the cue to pause and reach for professional support.

### The pre-course self-screen (plain language; v2 rewrite)

v1 phrased this screen in course vocabulary ("unable to center, ground, drop a bubble, return to Adult"), which a newcomer has not yet learned. The plain version a prospective learner can actually answer:

A person should **wait** to take this course, take it slowly with active professional support alongside, or take it later, if right now any of these is true:

- You are in a mental health crisis, or a clinician has told you that you are close to one.
- Someone close to you died within the last 60 days (case by case; see the intake question below).
- You are in the active middle of a major life rupture: a separation, job loss, eviction, or a serious new diagnosis.
- When something upsets you strongly, you usually cannot settle your body again within an hour or two, alone or with a friend; strong feelings tend to take you over for a day or more.
- Difficult material from your past is already in motion (in therapy or on its own) and you have no therapist, counselor, or coach currently alongside you.

The CM applies the **full intake screening protocol** in [04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md](04%20-%20Container%20and%20Gatekeeping%20Protocol.md) §A: five specific screens (suicidality, trauma and dissociation, addiction, coercive environment, mania/psychosis) with explicit thresholds and actions, plus the 18+ line and the bereavement question. The screening is the gate, not a recommendation.

### The bereavement / active-rupture screen (v2 addition: the 60-day rule made operational)

v1 stated the 60-day default here and in Module 6 but never asked at intake. v2 asks. The intake form carries one Y/N question (form wording lives in Doc 04 §A.1):

> *In the last 60 days, has someone close to you died, or has a major part of your life come apart (separation, job loss, eviction, serious diagnosis)?*

**Y →** a 1:1 CM consult before access, and the **stretched cadence as the default offer**, not an option the learner has to discover. The consult is routing, not denial: some recent losses coexist well with this work when support is in place; the CM checks that it is. **N →** continue.

---

## B. The four working agreements

A learner who begins Module 1 is agreeing to four things. They are repeated at the start of every module.

1. **Choice is always available.** The learner can pause, skip a practice, slow down, take a longer break between modules, or stop entirely. Nothing in the course is mandatory. No PM teacher anywhere requires a learner to "push through" anything. If the learner's body says no, the answer is no.
2. **Consent is ongoing.** The course asks the learner's consent at the start of each high-intensity practice. Consent given at enrollment does not carry forward into every exercise. The learner can withdraw consent for any single practice and continue with the course.
3. **The learner is responsible for their own state.** The course teaches the practices: center, ground, drop a bubble, feel, choose, ask, declare. The learner practices using them. A facilitator is not present in real time. If the learner cannot access the practices in a given moment, they pause and reach for support: partner, CM, or the referral list.
4. **The witness is alive.** Before starting Modules 5, 6, 7 and 9, the learner confirms their partner (or recruited witness) is reachable. If not, the module waits. This is not a suggestion. It is a structural requirement.

---

## C. Intensity tags

Every module is tagged Low / Medium / High. The tag is in the header block and drives pacing and check-in requirements.

| Tag | Modules | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| **Low** | 0, 1, 3, 4, 10, 11 | Largely cognitive and embodied-light. May surface feeling but is unlikely to put the learner in unfamiliar territory. No required partner check-in before the module. **Module 3 is Low *emotional* intensity but structurally load-bearing: it installs the center/ground/bubble/cube equipment every High module depends on. Do not skim it.** |
| **Medium** | 2, 8 | Module 2: disidentification + Box inventory + survival-layer preview. Module 8: direct relational practice on the learner's actual material; for many, as intense as the feelings work. **Partner check-in before starting is named in the module header; debrief after.** |
| **High** | 5, 6, 7, 9 | Directly engages the emotional body, drama patterns, ego states, or the Gremlin. May surface material the learner has not seen before. **Required partner check-in before; required debrief after. Gated by the unlock checklist in Doc 04 §D.** High-module headers also link the re-entry page (§O) and `ground.html`. |

There is **no skip-after-24-hours override** for the High-module check-in. The earlier framework permitted it; it is retracted. If a learner cannot meet the unlock checklist, they wait. The course will still be there in a week.

**Modules 5–9 are the climb** and the course says so out loud: four High modules and a heavy Medium, cushioned by the integration pause after Module 6 and the rest day between Modules 8 and 9. The pause beats are part of the safety architecture, not scheduling advice.

---

## D. The universal grounding script

Available at the top of every module file as a callout box, and shipped standalone as **[`Interactive Tools/ground.html`](Interactive%20Tools/ground.html)**: bookmarkable, one tap from every gated tool's GROUND NOW button. Reproduced here:

> **Grounding (60 seconds).**
> 1. Stop whatever you were doing in the module. Close the video or the document.
> 2. Stand up. Both feet on the floor. Knees soft.
> 3. Breathe out longer than you breathe in, three times. Audible exhale is fine.
> 4. Name three things you can see in the room.
> 5. Name one thing you can hear.
> 6. Place one hand on your sternum, the other on your belly. Notice that your body is here.
> 7. When you have come back, decide: continue, pause for 10 minutes, or stop the module for today.

This script is the default action whenever a learner notices any of the following:
- Feeling far away from themselves
- Inability to follow the words on the page or in the video
- Heart racing without clear cause
- The sense that they are watching themselves from outside
- Crying that does not feel like a clean feeling but feels stuck
- Strong urge to flee, attack, or shut down

These are signals to ground first, then decide. They are not signals that something is wrong with the learner. They are signals that the nervous system needs a beat.

---

## E. When to pause the module

The course teaches a clean distinction:

- **Discomfort that is moving** → keep going gently. This is the work.
- **Discomfort that is stuck or escalating** → ground (Section D), then pause for at least 10 minutes, then re-decide.
- **Discomfort that is unmistakably crisis** → stop, contact the partner, and if needed the referral list (Section H).

The learner does not have to perfectly classify their state. The default action when uncertain is to ground and pause. The course losing 10 minutes to caution is fine. The learner pushing through a state that needed a pause is not.

---

## F. When to call the partner

The partner pairing is not just for the structured exchanges. It is also a real-time resource. A learner sends the partner a voice message — short, no production — in any of these situations:

- They notice they have been in a stuck feeling for more than 24 hours
- They notice they have been numbing (substances, screens, work, food) more than usual since the module
- They had a strong reaction during the module and want to be witnessed
- They are about to do something significant in their life that the module surfaced (a difficult conversation, leaving a job, ending a relationship) — partner is witness, not advisor
- They notice they are stalling on the next module and want to talk about why

Partners do not advise. Partners witness, reflect, and ask the questions taught in Modules 8 and 9. If the partner is over their head, **the partner contacts the CM**, not the learner's professional support. The CM decides whether to escalate.

### Partner immediate-danger protocol

> **If your partner tells you they may harm themselves or someone else, ask: "Are you in immediate danger right now?"**
>
> If **yes** — contact local emergency services or your country's crisis line AND the CM **immediately.** Do not wait for course protocol. Do not wait for the CM's 24-hour SLA. Stay with your partner in the message thread until they have connected with crisis services.
>
> If **no** — voice-message the CM the same day. The CM will reach out to your partner directly.

This rule is item 4 of the 7-line agreement every partner and every recruited witness signs (Doc 04 §B). A person who has not signed it is not eligible for the witness role. This section, the Module 6 witness scripts, and the **Partner Emergency Card** (Facilitator Resources safety pack) together form the witness annex a recruited witness reads before any High module (Doc 04 §B2). Solo readers without a witness route to the defer-list instead (§P).

---

## G. When to call the CM

The CM is reachable by direct message with a guaranteed reply within 24 hours **for routine messages.** For active crisis (red flag in the triage rubric — Doc 04 §F), the standard is **immediate on detection, with first response within 15 minutes** of the CM (or named backup) seeing the message, crisis line numbers in that first reply. The CM ensures a named backup with accepted role + platform access + crisis protocol covers any window when the CM is unreachable.

A learner reaches the CM when:

- They want to pause the cohort for personal reasons
- Their partner is unresponsive or the pairing is not working
- Something happened in their life since enrollment that affects whether they should continue
- They are concerned about another learner in the cohort
- They want a referral
- They are considering stopping the course (see §O; stopping well is a held path, and the CM holds it)

The CM does **not** provide coaching or therapy. The CM helps the learner make a structural decision and points to resources.

---

## H. The referral pathway

The template list lives in [Facilitator Resources/Referral List.md](Facilitator%20Resources/Referral%20List.md). The operational artifact is the **per-learner referral packet** generated from intake (Doc 04 §E) and confirmed before content access opens. Crisis lines are listed first, never buried. **France's line is 3114** (national, 24/7) in every artifact where crisis lines appear: the Referral List, the per-learner packets, and any tool or page that names lines.

**The CM refers when:**
- A learner expresses suicidal ideation, intent or plan → crisis line first, then licensed clinician
- A learner is processing material clearly beyond the course's scope (active trauma, abuse history, addiction) → licensed clinician
- A learner wants 1:1 PM-aware support beyond the partner pairing → PM-aware coach
- A learner is well and wants to deepen → PM Trainer Path or local PM community

**Referral language template (CM, when needed):**
> What I'm hearing is bigger than what the course is built to hold. That doesn't mean you're doing the work wrong — it means the work you're doing right now needs someone qualified to be with you in it. Here are three options: [coach / clinician / line]. The course will be here when you come back. Do you want me to put the cohort on pause for you?

---

## I. UNIFIED CONSENT SPEC (v2: one component, one behavior, everywhere)

v1 had the right consent stance and one hole (a tool that persisted consent forever) and one side door (an ungated Atlas EHP). v2 unifies consent into a single specification that every gated surface implements. The spec is binding on all current and future tools:

1. **Per-visit, never persisted.** The consent block re-arms on every page load. No consent state is ever written to localStorage, cookies, or anywhere else. There is no "remember my choice." Consent to survival-layer or High-intensity work is a present-tense act; yesterday's yes is data, not consent.
2. **Day-specific items.** Each gated surface names what *this* practice engages (feelings work, the EHP, drama patterns, ego states, the survival layer) and the day-specific confirmations, not a generic checkbox.
3. **Real gating (`inert`).** Until consent is given, the practice content is programmatically inert: not focusable, not readable by shortcut, not scrolled past. The gate is structural, not decorative.
4. **GROUND NOW always reachable from the gate.** The grounding script (§D / `ground.html`) is available *at* the consent screen, not only behind it. A learner deciding whether to consent may already need it.
5. **No skip-override.** There is no "I'll confirm later" path. Declining is a full, honored choice: the page offers the locator-level alternative or simply today's exit.

**The learner-facing consent prompt** (each High module's header, verbatim core):

> **Consent check.**
> This module engages [feelings / drama patterns / ego states / etc.] directly. Material that has been managed by avoidance may surface. You are choosing to do this.
>
> Before continuing, confirm to yourself:
> - My partner (or recruited witness) is reachable today and tomorrow.
> - I have at least 90 uninterrupted minutes ahead of me.
> - I am not in acute crisis or under the influence right now.
> - I know how to ground (see top of file).
> - I know how to reach the CM if I need to.
>
> If any of the above is not true, pause this module and come back when it is.
> If all of the above is true and you choose to continue, take a slow breath and begin.

**Implementing tools, named:** the Module 2 survival-layer shield in `Interactive Tools/Day 02/box-inventory.html`; the High-day gates in `Day 05/feeling-locator.html`, `Day 06/ehp-walker.html`, `Day 07/drama-detector.html`, and `Day 09/ego-state-locator.html`; and the Atlas: **`Map Atlas/M12` carries no live EHP walk-through; it describes the process and routes to the gated Day 6 walker.** Solo-reader variants of the Module 5/6/7/9 consent blocks carry the "Reading this without a cohort?" box (§P). Any new gated tool implements all five points or does not ship; the Manifest's no-consent-key rule (Manifest §6) is the regression check.

This is not bureaucratic. It is the consent moment that distinguishes a chosen practice from a thing happening to the learner.

---

## J. Special-population notes

- **Survivors of trauma.** PM is precise that *emotional healing process work* (in scope) is distinct from *trauma processing* (not in scope). Survivors should be in concurrent care with a trauma-qualified clinician and check with that clinician about whether to take the course now or later. The CM does not screen these clinically.
- **Learners on psychiatric medication.** PM does not contraindicate medication. The course does not advise changing medication. Any medication question goes to the prescribing physician.
- **Neurodivergent learners.** The course's pacing assumes the learner can self-regulate length-of-engagement and break frequency; the sitting markers help, and the stretched cadence (Doc 02 §K) is freely available. The CM asks, not assumes.
- **Learners in current grief.** Recent loss + this course can be productive or destabilizing depending on the loss, the time elapsed, and the support system. Default: wait at least 60 days post-loss and have a therapist or grief-aware coach alongside. The intake question in §A makes this operational instead of aspirational.

---

## K. CM safety practices (for the CM, not the learner)

- **Read every cohort-feed post within 48 hours.** No exceptions. (24 hours during High windows.)
- **Log direct-message exchanges** (date, learner, summary, action taken). Brief is fine. The log protects the learner and the CM.
- **Keep the referral list current.** Quarterly check that listed clinicians and coaches are still practicing and accepting clients.
- **Refer without flinching.** A CM who delays referral because they want to "help first" is contributing to harm.
- **Hold confidentiality.** Cohort-feed posts are not shared outside the cohort. Direct-message exchanges are not shared with the learner's partner or other learners. The only exceptions: imminent risk of harm, legal compulsion, or the learner's own explicit consent.
- **Have a CM-side check-in.** The CM should themselves have a supervisor, peer, or coach to debrief with. Holding a cohort is real work.

---

## L. Incident response (if something goes wrong)

If a learner experiences a serious adverse event during or attributable to the course (psychiatric crisis, hospitalization, significant rupture):

1. **CM contacts the learner** — immediately if active crisis (red flag); within 24 hours otherwise. Plain language. Asks what happened, what they need, whether they are safe right now.
2. **CM offers** pause, refund (safety-routed exits always refund in full, Doc 05 Step 0), referral, or continued cohort access. The learner chooses.
3. **CM documents** the incident factually and confidentially.
4. **CM debriefs** with their own supervisor or peer.
5. **CM reviews** the course content the learner had been engaging with. If a pattern emerges across multiple learners with the same module, the module is revised before the next cohort.

This is not a defensive posture. It is the minimum honest response to material that engages people's inner lives.

---

## M. Three things this document does not pretend

1. **It does not pretend the course is safe in all cases.** It is safe for most adults who self-select into a thoughtware course and engage with reasonable care. It is not appropriate for everyone. The screening, the pacing controls and the referral pathway exist because of this.
2. **It does not pretend the CM is a therapist.** A CM holding a cohort with this framework is doing real and skilled work, but it is the work of a community caretaker, not a clinician.
3. **It does not pretend that doing nothing is also safe.** Many of the learners drawn to this course are already in pain — pain that the absence of PM-grade thoughtware was not preventing. The decision to take the course (or to recommend it) is always weighed against the alternative of continuing without it.

---

## N. The next-morning check after every High module (v2 addition)

The morning after completing Module 5, 6, 7 or 9, before the daily sit and before opening anything new, the learner answers three questions in the Beep! Book. One line each:

1. **Body:** *Where is my body this morning: settled, tender, or still charged?*
2. **Overnight:** *Did yesterday's material keep moving in the night (dreams, looping thoughts, waking numbness)?*
3. **Need:** *What do I need before any new content: an ordinary day, a partner message, or a CM check-in?*

Routing is built into the answers: *settled / no / ordinary day* → proceed normally. *Tender or charged*, or *yes* on overnight → run the grounding script, take a no-new-content day, and send the partner one line. Anything still charged at the **second** morning → message the CM (that is the §F "stuck more than 24 hours" signal, caught early). The check takes under two minutes and is listed in each High module's flow close, after Script 9.

---

## O. Exit ramps and re-entry (v2 addition)

**Stopping well is a held path, not a failure mode.** The course says this to learners and equips the CM to hold it:

- **Learner-facing:** Module 11 carries the "stopping well" half-page: how to close the partner exchange cleanly, what to keep (the spine sit, the Beep! Book), how to leave the door open. It applies whether the learner stops at Module 4 or finishes everything.
- **CM-facing:** the **CM Leaver Checklist** (Facilitator Resources safety pack) sequences a clean exit: closure exchange offered, partner re-matched or released, access and refund handled per Doc 05 Step 0, referral packet re-sent, and a **leaver wellbeing check** (one scheduled CM message ~2 weeks after the exit, no reply required, logged metadata-only).
- **Re-entry:** [`Practice/Coming Back.md`](Practice/Coming%20Back.md) is the learner's return path (3+/7+/30+ day tiers, anti-binge rule, the return framed as a rep). It is linked from `START HERE.md` and from **every High-module header**, because the days after a High module are where silent absence most often starts.

---

## P. Solo-reader policy (v2 addition)

Per ARCHITECTURE decision 3 and Doc 02 §G: solo readers are sanctioned, the gates are intact. Safety-relevant specifics:

- Low and Medium modules are fully open solo. The High modules require a witness: either **recruit-a-witness** (a friend qualifies; protocol and disqualifiers in Doc 04 §B2; the witness reads §F's annex, the Module 6 witness script, and the Partner Emergency Card) or the **defer-list** (read the teaching, run locator-level practices, defer the EHP and Demon-adjacent work until witnessed).
- Solo readers have no CM and no SLA. The "Reading this without a cohort?" boxes say so plainly and name [findahelpline.com](https://findahelpline.com) as the referral floor.
- The defer-list is a boundary, not a workaround. A solo reader who self-runs the EHP against it is outside the course's design, and the course says that in advance rather than discovering it after.

---

## Q. The Screen-4 variant system (v2 addition)

Doc 04's Screen 4 (coercive or unsafe environment) promises low-stakes experiment substitutions; v2 builds them. **Every between-module experiment in the [Experiment Bank](Facilitator%20Resources/Experiment%20Bank.md) carries a lower-stakes variant column**: same distinction trained, no direct confrontation with the flagged person, no action that could touch safety, housing, children, or income.

Modules 1, 7, 8 and 10 (the modules whose default experiments are most confrontation-shaped) carry a **discreet learner-facing cue**, one line, no flag visible to anyone else: *"If a direct version of this experiment could put your safety, housing, or income at risk, run the variant column version. That is the course working, not you opting out."* The CM substitutes variants silently for Screen-4-flagged learners at experiment touchpoints; the learner's record shows nothing in the feed.

---
