---

# CM First-Cohort Run-Book

| | |
|---|---|
| **For** | A qualified-but-new Community Manager (CM) running their first async Expand the Box cohort |
| **When** | Open at Week 0 (before any learner is enrolled) and work it top to bottom through cohort close |
| **Turns into a path** | What Doc 04 holds as a *bar* (qualifications, checklists, rubrics), this turns into a *sequence* — what you do, in what order |
| **Reads alongside** | `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` (the binding protocol — this run-book operationalizes it) · `03 - Safety and Facilitation Framework.md` Sections F, G, K, L · `02 - Async Delivery Framework.md` Sections C, D, F · `Async Partner Pairing Playbook.md` · `Cohort Feed Templates.md` |

This is not a re-statement of the protocol. Doc 04 is the law; this is the route through it. Where a thing is fully specified elsewhere, this file points to the section and tells you *when* in the sequence to do it — it does not duplicate it. Read the cross-referenced sections; do not assume the one-line pointer is the whole instruction.

Before you start: confirm your own qualifications are met (Doc 04 §J). If your clinical supervisor is not yet named and contracted, and your liability insurance is not in force, you are not ready to open a cohort. Stop here and close those first. Templates for both agreements are in `CM Agreement Templates.md`.

---

## The shape of the run (one screen)

```
WEEK 0      Self-readiness · platform stack · supervisor + backup CM contracted
WEEK 0      Intake opens → screen every applicant (Doc 04 §A) → route green/defer/1:1
WEEK 0–1    Referral packet per learner (Doc 04 §E) · partner matching (Playbook §1)
WEEK 0–1    Partner agreements signed · platforms chosen · pairing tracker built
PRE-LAUNCH  Pre-cohort checklist sign-off (Doc 04 §K) — every box, or no open
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MODULES     Per-module cadence: read feed ≤48h · triage every post · tracker check
HIGH WEEKS  Days 5, 6, 7, 9 — extra-vigilance checklist before/during/after
ALWAYS      Backup CM covers any window you're unreachable · red = immediate/15-min
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CLOSE       Day 10 closure · pairing close-out · feed gratitude (Feed Templates §7)
POST        Post-cohort review 1–2 weeks after Day 10 (Doc 04 §L) · 1 improvement
```

Everything below expands one band of this strip.

---

## Part 1 — Pre-launch sequence (Week 0 → content opens)

Do these in order. Each step has a *done-when* you can check off. Nothing downstream opens until the step above it is closed.

### Step 1 — Confirm your own readiness (before any learner sees a form)

Doc 04 §J is the bar. Run it on yourself first.

- [ ] Expand the Box (this or equivalent live ETB) completed, plus one further PM training
- [ ] Current mental-health first-aid (or country-recognized equivalent) certification in date
- [ ] Clinical supervisor named, contracted, and reachable for red-flag consultation (agreement template in `CM Agreement Templates.md`; this is the consultation agreement Doc 04 §J requires)
- [ ] Written CM–Operator agreement signed (scope, liability, confidentiality exceptions — template in `CM Agreement Templates.md`)
- [ ] Professional liability insurance in force for your jurisdiction (or you are employed by an entity that carries it)
- [ ] You have re-read Doc 04 §F (triage) and §G (crisis escalation) within the last 30 days

**Done-when:** every box above is checked. If any is open, you are not a qualified CM for this cohort yet (Doc 04 §J — "a cohort run by an underqualified CM is a cohort running without a safety framework"). Close the gap before Step 2.

### Step 2 — Stand up the platform stack

Pick the stack, then test each component against the privacy minimums in Doc 04 §H **before** intake opens — not after learners arrive.

- [ ] Document hosting: access-controlled, not publicly indexed, deletion-on-request supported
- [ ] Cohort feed: closed to the cohort, not externally visible, author can delete, CM can hide
- [ ] 1:1 CM channel: private, metadata-only logging
- [ ] Voice-message tool the partners will use meets §H (you don't pick it for them, but you publish the acceptable list: Telegram / WhatsApp E2EE / Voxer / Signal — never Discord servers, group chats, or anything that auto-transcribes)
- [ ] The private spreadsheet that will hold the pairing tracker (Doc 04 §C) and triage log (Feed Templates §5) exists, and only you and your supervisor can open it

**Done-when:** every component passes §H, or you've replaced it / shrunk the cohort per §H's fallback ("cohort feed becomes a shared doc the CM moderates manually").

### Step 3 — Open intake and screen every applicant

Intake is the gate, not a survey (Doc 04 §A). Every prospective learner completes the 12-minute form before content access is even discussed.

For each applicant:

- [ ] Identity, cadence, region, language, availability captured (Doc 04 §A.1) — you need region and language to generate the referral packet, and cadence + time-zone window to match partners
- [ ] The five screens applied with their thresholds (Doc 04 §A.2): suicidality · trauma & dissociation · addiction · coercive environment · mania/psychosis
- [ ] Each applicant routed to exactly one of three outcomes:
  - **Green → continue.** All five screens returned "none of the above" (or the explicitly-allowed in-care variants).
  - **1:1 consult required → book it before access.** Any screen hit a "CM 1:1 consult" threshold, or the applicant answered "I'd rather not say." Remember the learner-facing framing: "no" or "rather not say" routes to a conversation, **not** automatic denial.
  - **Defer → no access now, re-application path given.** Any screen hit a defer/clinician-confirmation threshold (e.g., current suicidal plan/intent; monthly-or-more dissociation or lost time; uncontrolled addiction; mania/psychosis in past 12 months). Send the relevant referral-list portion and the re-application language from Doc 04 §A.2.
- [ ] Coercive-environment "yes" (Screen 4) flagged on the learner's record now — it changes their referral packet (adds DV/legal resources) and substitutes their between-module experiments with low-stakes variants (Doc 04 §A.2, Screen 4)
- [ ] Acknowledgments completed (Doc 04 §A.3 — six initials, including "this is not therapy" and "I will pair before Day 1")

**Done-when:** every applicant is green-with-acknowledgments, scheduled for a 1:1, or deferred-with-resources. No one moves to matching until they are green and acknowledged.

> First-cohort note. Do the screening yourself, slowly, applicant by applicant. Do not batch it or delegate the threshold judgment to a form's auto-logic on your first run — you want to feel where the thresholds bite before you trust automation with them.

### Step 4 — Generate the referral packet per learner

For every green/acknowledged learner, generate the packet from their §A.1 inputs (Doc 04 §E). This is region-specific; a generic packet is not the artifact.

- [ ] Crisis line for the learner's country, listed **first**
- [ ] Licensed-clinician directory link for their region (brief if they already have a clinician)
- [ ] PM-aware coaches in their language, with rates
- [ ] DV + legal resources **only if** Screen 4 returned yes
- [ ] Not-therapy disclaimer paragraph
- [ ] Packet saved as the learner's downloadable PDF **and** kept on the platform

**Done-when:** a packet exists and is verified for every enrolled learner (Doc 04 §K bullet 2). The template list lives in `Referral List.md`; the per-learner packet is what you actually generate and confirm.

### Step 5 — Match partners

Matching happens before content opens (Doc 04 §A.1 routing feeds this; full method in `Async Partner Pairing Playbook.md` §1). Cadence first, time-zone within 6h, shared language, no close ties.

- [ ] Every learner matched (odd cohort: you bridge the leftover learner for week one only — Playbook §1, "Solo learners")
- [ ] Welcome email sent to each pair (Playbook §2), partner agreement (Doc 04 §B) attached, referral packet re-attached
- [ ] Both partners in each pair have replied "signed" to the 7-line partner agreement — **a learner who will not acknowledge item 4 (immediate-danger protocol) is not eligible** (Doc 04 §B)
- [ ] Each pair has chosen a §H-compliant platform and confirmed it to you
- [ ] Pairing tracker rows created for every pair (Doc 04 §C — metadata only, never content)

**Done-when:** every learner has a signed partner and a platform. Silent or unsigned pairings are not "almost ready" — they are not ready (Playbook §1).

### Step 6 — Pre-cohort checklist sign-off (the gate to open)

This is Doc 04 §K, verbatim in intent. You personally sign it. If any line is unchecked, the cohort does not open.

- [ ] Every enrolled learner passed intake and acknowledged consent (Step 3)
- [ ] Every learner has a region-specific referral packet on file (Step 4)
- [ ] Every learner is paired (Step 5)
- [ ] Both partners in each pair signed the partner agreement (Step 5)
- [ ] Pairing tracker is set up (Step 2 + Step 5)
- [ ] CM qualifications are met (Step 1)
- [ ] **Backup CM contact is identified and has accepted the role, with platform access and the crisis protocol in hand** (see Part 4 — this is the line new CMs most often leave half-done)
- [ ] Platform stack meets privacy minimums (Step 2)
- [ ] Cohort feed rules posted and pinned (Feed Templates §1 welcome post)
- [ ] Crisis escalation playbook (Doc 04 §F, §G) reviewed by you in the past 30 days (Step 1)

**Done-when:** all ten checked. Open content access. Not before (Doc 04 §K — "if any item is unchecked, the cohort does not open").

---

## Part 2 — Per-module CM cadence (Day 1 → Day 10)

The rhythm repeats every module. Doc 02 §A is the learner's week; this is *yours*.

### The standing weekly loop (every module)

1. **Post the module's feed prompt** at module start (Feed Templates §2 — one or two sentences, signal not story).
2. **Read every cohort-feed post within 48 hours.** No exceptions (Doc 03 §K). On High-module weeks, tighten to 24h for the mid-course check-in window (Feed Templates §6).
3. **Triage every post and DM** into green/yellow/red the moment you read it (Doc 04 §F). Log metadata only (Feed Templates §5).
4. **Check the pairing tracker** against its mechanical triggers (Doc 04 §C): missed SLA twice in one module → DM both; missed in two consecutive modules → offer re-match; one partner silent 6 days → offer the responsive partner a re-match. The triggers do the remembering for you; verbatim DM language is in Playbook §4.
5. **Answer routine DMs within 24h** (Doc 03 §G). Red flags are not routine — see Part 3.
6. **Hold the feed narrow.** Redirect drift (confessional, advice, theory debate) privately using the verbatim language in Feed Templates §4.

That is the whole per-module job in calm water. Most weeks are calm water. The triage rubric is what you reach for when a post is *not* obviously calm.

### Applying the triage rubric — what the three flags mean

Straight from Doc 04 §F. Hold these definitions exactly; the response window is the load-bearing part.

| Flag | Looks like | Window | Your action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Green** | Normal reflection, "this is hard," frustration with a concept, ordinary doubt | None required | Acknowledgment optional |
| **Yellow** | Stuck feeling >24h · increased numbing reported · partner conflict surfacing · "over my head but functional" · a life stressor compounding the work | **DM within 24h** | Offer voice call, pause, slower cadence, or 1:1 referral; log it |
| **Red** | Suicidal ideation (any) · imminent harm to self/others · abuse in progress · psychotic/dissociative state in progress · medical emergency | **Same day, immediate** | Crisis-line numbers in the first message; hand off to clinician/emergency; do not "discuss" — refer (Part 3); log; debrief supervisor |

When uncertain between two flags, **classify up** (treat a maybe-yellow as yellow, a maybe-red as red) and let the conversation tell you. The course losing nothing to caution is fine; a missed red is not.

### Worked examples — ambiguous feed posts and how to classify + respond

These are the posts that don't sort themselves. Use them to calibrate your eye on the first cohort. Each shows the post, the call, the reasoning, and the move.

**Worked example A — "I've gone numb again" (Day 5).**

> *Post:* "Day 5. Honestly I came in wanting to feel more and this module I just went flat. Watched the whole thing from behind glass. Couldn't find a single feeling under the Numbness Bar. Kind of done with trying."

- **Call: Yellow.** Increased numbing reported + "kind of done" reads as deflation, not crisis. There is no harm indicator. But "went flat / behind glass" is the dissociation-adjacent language Doc 03 §D names, so it is past green.
- **Why not green:** "behind glass" and "watched from outside" are on Doc 03 §D's grounding-trigger list; numbness deepening across a High module is a yellow stuck-state signal, not ordinary doubt.
- **Why not red:** no suicidal ideation, no harm, no psychotic/dissociative state *in progress at the moment of writing* — they wrote a coherent reflection. If the next reply contained "and I don't see the point of any of it anymore," it crosses to red.
- **Move:** DM within 24h. "Saw your Day 5 post — going flat behind glass is exactly the Numbness Bar doing its job, not you failing the module. Before you decide you're done, two things: run the 60-second grounding script at the top of the file, and tell me — is the flatness only in the module, or has it been in your week too? Want a short voice call this week?" Offer pause/stretched cadence. Log Yellow. If "in my week too" comes back with lost time or not-functioning, re-triage toward defer-and-refer.

**Worked example B — the dark-but-completed past (Day 7).**

> *Post:* "Day 7. Caught my Persecutor clean for the first time. The move: I tear people down in meetings so I feel less small. Reminds me of a year a while back when I genuinely wanted to not be here anymore — glad that's behind me. Gremlin ate well off the meetings though."

- **Call: Yellow, with a same-day soft touch.** This is mostly a *good* Day 7 post — specific, self-located, gremlin-aware. But it contains a past suicidality disclosure ("wanted to not be here anymore"), explicitly framed as resolved ("glad that's behind me").
- **Why not red:** the ideation is reported in the past tense and self-framed as over. Red is for ideation that is *current or active* (Doc 04 §F). Treating every historical mention as red would punish honesty and teach learners not to disclose.
- **Why not plain green:** a suicidality mention — even past-tense — gets a private acknowledgment and a quiet check that "behind me" is actually behind them. You verify; you don't assume.
- **Move:** Acknowledge the Day 7 work *in the feed* if you'd normally acknowledge (keep it about the Persecutor catch — never surface the suicidality reference publicly). Then DM, same day, light: "Strong catch on the Persecutor today. You named something from a harder time too — I'm not making it a big thing, just checking: is that genuinely in the rear-view, and do you have someone in your life you'd reach for if it ever came back?" If the answer wobbles ("mostly… some days…"), you're now in yellow-trending-red: send the referral packet's clinician section and ask the Doc 03 §H pause question. Log it with the historical-disclosure note.

**Worked example C — the experiment that's about to cost something real (Day 1 / Day 2).**

> *Post:* "Radical responsibility landed hard. I've decided I'm done being a victim about my marriage — going to confront my husband this weekend and tell him everything that's wrong, no filter. Box has been running this for 15 years and I'm DONE."

- **Call: Yellow → check the intake flag immediately.** The post itself is energized, not in crisis. The risk is the *experiment*: a no-filter confrontation inspired by Day 1's responsibility content is exactly the move Doc 04 §A (Screen 4) and §72-style coercion logic exist to catch.
- **First action before responding:** check this learner's record for a Screen 4 (coercive/unsafe environment) flag. If Screen 4 returned **yes**, this is closer to red-adjacent — a course-inspired experiment is heading toward the exact risk the screen was meant to prevent, and you intervene firmly. If Screen 4 was clean, it's a yellow coaching-the-container moment.
- **Why not green:** Day 1 is explicitly the module that can send a fired-up learner into a real-life action that costs them (Doc 04 §A, Screen 4 note — "a learner inspired by Day 1's radical-responsibility content can run an experiment that costs them their housing"). The feed naming an imminent high-stakes confrontation is a structural yellow regardless of charge.
- **Move:** DM within 24h (same day if Screen 4 = yes). "Big landing — and I want to slow one thing down. Radical responsibility is a stance about *your* creating, not a green light for a no-filter confrontation. The course's experiments are deliberately small reps, not life-altering moves (that's by design). Before this weekend, let's talk: what's the smallest true version of what you want to say? If there's any way this conversation could put your safety, housing, or finances at risk, we substitute a low-stakes experiment — that's not me doubting you, it's the course refusing to create risk in your actual life." Point to the between-module experiment's small-rep framing. If Screen 4 = yes, also re-send the DV/legal resources from their packet. Log Yellow (or Red if Screen 4 = yes and risk is concrete).

The pattern across all three: **the flag is set by risk and state, not by emotional intensity.** A hot post about catching the Box is green-to-yellow; a calm post naming an imminent real-world risk is yellow-to-red. Intensity is the work (Doc 03 §M); risk is what you triage.

---

## Part 3 — When a Red flag arrives (the immediate path)

Red is not a 24-hour item. It is **immediate on detection, first response within 15 minutes** of you (or your named backup) seeing it, with crisis-line numbers in that first message (Doc 03 §G, Doc 04 §F). Run Doc 04 §G's sequence exactly:

1. **Acknowledge within 15 minutes.** "I see you. I'm here. Are you safe right now?"
2. **If immediate physical danger:** crisis-line number for their country in the message; ask "Will you call them now, or do you want me to stay on while you call?"; stay in the thread until they've connected with crisis services **or** 90 minutes pass and you contact your designated backup clinician.
3. **If red-flagged but not in immediate danger:** connect them to a PM-aware coach (if they have one), their existing clinician (with consent), or the licensed-clinician list from their packet; follow up within 24h to confirm the connection happened.
4. **Pause their cohort access** until they and their clinician agree it's appropriate to resume. Frame as care, not punishment.
5. **Document** (date, action, learner consent for any disclosure) and **debrief your supervisor within 72 hours.**

You do not provide clinical care at any step. Stay present, hand off, document (Doc 04 §G). The referral language template in Doc 03 §H is the words to use. If a partner is the one who surfaces the red flag, they follow the buddy immediate-danger protocol (Doc 03 §F) and relay to you — they are the relay, not the clinician (Playbook §9, scenario 5).

---

## Part 4 — High-module weeks (Days 5, 6, 7, 9): extra vigilance

High modules directly engage the emotional body, drama patterns, the gremlin, and ego states (Doc 03 §C). They are where material the learner hasn't seen before surfaces. Run this extra checklist *around* each High module, on top of the standing weekly loop.

### Before the module unlocks (per learner)

Verify the unlock checklist (Doc 04 §D) is satisfied — gate it programmatically where the platform can, manually where it can't:

- [ ] Partner agreement signed by both
- [ ] Partner was responsive on the prior module (no missed SLA)
- [ ] Learner confirmed: "My partner is reachable today and tomorrow" — and this is the *result of an actual exchange* 24–48h ahead (Playbook §7 script), not an assertion in their head
- [ ] Learner confirmed: "I have 90 uninterrupted minutes ahead of me"
- [ ] Learner confirmed: "I am not in acute crisis or under the influence right now"
- [ ] Learner has read this module's consent prompt (Doc 03 §I)
- [ ] **If Screen 1 (suicidality) or Screen 5 (mania) applied at enrollment: clinician confirmation is on file for *this* module** (Doc 04 §D — per-module, not once)
- [ ] Partners are not cadence-mismatched (Doc 04 §I — High modules do not unlock when partners are >1 module apart; either the faster waits, a witness partner is assigned per Playbook §6, or re-match)

**The skip-after-24h override is gone** (Doc 03 §C, Doc 04 §D). If a learner can't meet the gate, they wait. The course will still be there.

### During the High-module window

- [ ] Read the feed at **24h, not 48h**, for the duration of the window (the cohort is at higher charge)
- [ ] Send the lightweight High-week check-in DM to every learner ("how's it landing? anything to flag?") — no reply required (Doc 02 §F, "High-intensity weeks")
- [ ] Between Day 5 and Day 6 specifically, post the mid-course "what's alive" pause prompt (Feed Templates §6); read replies within 24h; any "too fast" triggers a cadence DM, any partner-related answer triggers a tracker check
- [ ] Watch for the Day-specific surfaces:
  - **Day 5 (Feelings vs Emotions / Numbness Bar):** numbness deepening, "behind glass," a learner naming emotions (story-attached, past) where the module asks for feelings (present, located) — the partner gently re-scopes and points to you (Playbook §3, Day 5 example)
  - **Day 6 (Mixed Emotions / Emotional Healing Process):** the most demanding module; async partner-reply latency can stretch its calendar time (Doc 02 §A) — make sure no learner is mid-process and waiting days for a witness
  - **Day 7 (Low Drama / Gremlin Food):** historical disclosures surfacing as learners catch Persecutor/Victim/Rescuer moves (Worked example B); drama patterns can route present feeling through old material
  - **Day 9 (Ego States):** archetypal and ego-state material can amplify mania/psychosis/dissociation (Doc 04 §A, Screen 5; Doc 01 §"Box layers") — this is the module the clinician-confirmation gate most protects

### After the module

- [ ] Confirm the required partner debrief happened (tracker shows the exchange — content stays private) (Doc 03 §C)
- [ ] Any learner who went quiet across a High module gets a direct check-in DM, not a wait-and-see

---

## Part 5 — Backup-CM handoff (how the 15-minute crisis SLA survives you being unreachable)

You will sleep, travel, lose signal, and have hours off. The red-flag SLA does not pause for any of that (Doc 03 §G — "the CM ensures a named backup with accepted role + platform access + crisis protocol covers any window when the CM is unreachable"). This is the procedure new CMs most often under-build. Build it before launch (it is a line on the §K gate).

### Who the backup is

- A specific named person who has **accepted the role** — not "someone I'll call." Ideally a fellow qualified CM, a PM-aware coach, or your clinical supervisor acting in a coverage capacity. The backup must themselves meet enough of the Doc 04 §J floor to hold a red flag: mental-health first aid, the ability to refer without flinching, and access to the crisis protocol.
- The backup is named on your enrollment paperwork and the platform privacy setup (Doc 04 §H lists the 1:1 channel; the backup needs read access to it for coverage windows).

### What the backup has, before they're ever needed

- [ ] **Accepted the role in writing** (a coverage clause in the CM–Operator agreement, or a short separate note)
- [ ] **Platform access** to the 1:1 CM channel and the cohort feed (so they can see and respond)
- [ ] **The crisis escalation flow** (Doc 04 §G) and **triage rubric** (Doc 04 §F) in hand
- [ ] **The current pairing tracker + triage log location** (read access) so they know the cohort's state
- [ ] **The referral packets** for the cohort's regions (so they can drop a region-correct crisis line into a first message)
- [ ] **Your supervisor's contact** as the 90-minute escalation point (Doc 04 §G step 2)

### How coverage is triggered

1. **Planned absence** (sleep window, travel, a known offline block): you tell the backup the window in advance and post nothing learner-facing — coverage is silent. If you run a cohort across incompatible time zones, name the nightly window the backup owns.
2. **Unplanned unreachability:** define the trip-wire in advance. The standard is — if a learner's red-flag message sits unacknowledged for **10 minutes**, the platform/learner routing (or the backup's own feed-watch) escalates to the backup, who then owns the 15-minute first-response SLA. The 5-minute buffer between the 10-minute trip-wire and the 15-minute SLA is deliberate.
3. **The backup runs Doc 04 §G exactly as you would** — acknowledge ≤15 min, crisis line in the first message, stay present, hand off, document. They log it in the shared triage log so the record is continuous.
4. **Hand-back:** when you're reachable again, the backup briefs you on anything that moved; you debrief your supervisor within 72h on any red flag the backup handled (Doc 04 §G step 5 — the debrief is yours even if the backup ran the call).

### Test it once, before Day 1

Send the backup a drill message ("this is a test — walk me through your first three actions if a red flag arrives in your window"). If they can't name *acknowledge ≤15 min → region crisis line in first message → stay until connected or 90-min supervisor escalation*, they are not ready, and your §K gate is not actually met.

> The single most common first-cohort failure mode is treating the backup as a formality — a name on a form with no access and no drill. A backup without platform access cannot meet a 15-minute SLA. Build the access and run the drill, or you do not have a backup.

---

## Part 6 — Cohort close and post-cohort review

### Closing the cohort (Day 10 → close)

- [ ] Day 10 closure exchange happens between partners (Playbook §8 — explicit closure, not fade)
- [ ] Opt-in continuation message sent to each partner separately (Playbook §8) — item 7 ends with the cohort; flag it
- [ ] Feed gratitude prompts posted (Feed Templates §7): partner-to-partner, then partner-to-cohort; announce the feed's read-only archive date
- [ ] Learners reminded how to access materials post-cohort (Doc 02 §F, "Closure")
- [ ] Any learner whose access was paused mid-cohort (red flag) is closed out per their own clinician timeline, not the cohort calendar

### The post-cohort review (1–2 weeks after Day 10)

This is Doc 04 §L — a private review you conduct, then share with the operator and your supervisor. Pull from the triage log (Feed Templates §5) and pairing tracker (Doc 04 §C), both anonymized.

- [ ] **Flag distribution:** how many green / yellow / red, by module. Where did the charge cluster?
- [ ] **Content patterns:** where did multiple learners get stuck, ungrounded, or overwhelmed? (A pattern on one module = revise that module before the next cohort — Doc 03 §L step 5)
- [ ] **Pairing outcomes:** which matches worked, which needed re-matching, any pattern (cadence mismatch? time-zone? a recurring drift point?)
- [ ] **Learning-axis signal:** fold in the anonymized aggregate from `Learning Self-Assessment.md` — the baseline→Day-10 deltas and the per-HIGH-module readiness self-checks. Where learners report a distinction "not yet landed" at Day 10, or repeatedly flag a readiness gap before a High module, that is a content signal, not a learner failure. (See that file's closing note on how the aggregate feeds this review.)
- [ ] **One concrete improvement** proposed for the next cohort. One. Specific. Implementable.

Share with the operator and supervisor. Patterns recur across cohorts; the review is how the course improves (Doc 04 §L). It is also where your first cohort stops being a one-off and becomes the first data point in a system that compounds.

---

## First-cohort reminders (pin these)

- **The gate is the gate.** Intake screening, partner signing, the §K checklist, the High-module unlock — none of these is a suggestion you can soften when a keen applicant pushes. The protocol is required (Doc 04 status note).
- **Classify up when unsure.** Maybe-yellow → yellow. Maybe-red → red. Caution costs minutes; a missed red costs more.
- **Refer without flinching** (Doc 03 §K). Wanting to "help first" before referring is how a CM contributes to harm.
- **You are not the teacher and not a therapist** (Doc 02 §F, Doc 03 §M). You keep the container intact. The modules teach; clinicians treat; you triage, hand off, and document.
- **Metadata only, always.** The tracker and the log record *that* something happened and *what you did* — never what was said (Doc 04 §C, Feed Templates §5). Confidentiality holds except the three exceptions (Doc 03 §K).
- **The backup is real or you have no backup.** Access + drill, before Day 1.

---

---

# CM Leaver Checklist

*Operator-facing, half a page. Run it whenever a learner stops, silently or by telling you. Mechanics live in the Async Partner Pairing Playbook (§4–6, §9); this is the order of operations.*

---

## If the leave is silent

- [ ] **Chase at most twice.** Two voice messages, spaced about three days (the Playbook's SLA triggers do the timing). Then stop. The silence is data; respect it. No third message, no guilt copy.
- [ ] **Protect the partner.** At six days without an initiated exchange, re-match the responsive partner (Playbook Trigger 4; the default, not an escalation).
- [ ] **Close the pairing without blame.** The leaver stays formally in the cohort; access stays open; no removal email, no announcement. If the leaver resurfaces, route them to re-entry terms below.

## If the leave is declared

- [ ] **Leaver wellbeing check** (voice message or call, this script or close to it):

  > "Thank you for telling me — that is stopping well, not failing. Two questions before I close things cleanly. One: are you stopping because life is full, or because something in the course material knocked you over? If it's the second, tell me which module and I'll point you at the right support; that's my job, not a judgment. Two: is anything open right now (a stirred-up emotion, an unfinished exchange) that needs a hand before you put this down?"

  Anything Red in the answers (ideation, abuse disclosure, crisis) → triage rubric Red: same-day response, crisis lines in the first message.
- [ ] **Offer the pause before the close.** "Pause and resume next cohort" is on the table explicitly; the red-pill ceremony promised it (*"I can stop at any time"*). The course will still be here in three months — say so.
- [ ] **Close their pairing without blame.** Tell the remaining partner only the structure: the pairing is closed, a re-match is coming, nothing about why. Use the Playbook's no-story language; confidentiality holds across the boundary.

## Both paths

- [ ] **Stopping-well framing, said out loud.** Stopping well is a valid use of the container, not a failure of it. A learner who leaves cleanly returns at roughly the rate of one who never left badly; a learner shamed at the exit does not return at all.
- [ ] **Archive what gets archived.** Pairing-tracker row marked closed (structure only; the CM never logged exchange content). Cohort-feed posts stay until the standard 14-day post-cohort archive. The referral packet stays delivered. Nothing the learner would need for re-entry is deleted.
- [ ] **State the re-entry terms at leaving time**, so coming back is concrete, not vague goodwill:
  - The learner-facing path is [Coming Back](Practice/Coming%20Back.md) (3+/7+/30+ day tiers, anti-binge rule, High-module rule).
  - If their pairing was closed, re-entry runs through the **witness bench**: they witness one exchange before they are witnessed (Playbook §9: the ghost re-earns the container's trust by witnessing first). A declared leaver who stopped well re-enters on the same bench terms if their partner has been re-matched; nothing else is re-earned.
- [ ] **Log it for the post-cohort review:** leave date, module reached, silent/declared, re-entry terms stated. Structure, not story.

---

<!-- ===== source: Facilitator Resources/Cohort Calendar Template.md ===== -->

---

# Cohort Calendar Template

| | |
|---|---|
| **For** | The operator and CM, planning one cohort end to end |
| **What it is** | A fill-in calendar covering Modules 00–11, the pause beats, teach-backs, the mid-course call, and the 30/60/90 alumni touchpoints, with every SLA-relevant date marked for the CM |
| **Reads alongside** | `02 - Async Delivery Framework.md` (cadence) · `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` (the gates this calendar schedules) · [CM First-Cohort Run-Book](CM%20First-Cohort%20Run-Book.md) · [Cohort Feed Templates](Cohort%20Feed%20Templates.md) |

**How to use it.** Pick a start date (the day content access opens; a Monday works well so the heaviest stretch avoids landing on a learner's worst workdays). Fill the "Your dates" column top to bottom. The "Worked example" column shows a cohort opening **Monday 5 October 2026** at the recommended default cadence. Give every learner the filled calendar in the welcome packet, and pin it in the cohort feed.

**The cadence this template assumes.** Default pace: one module per **~3 days** (Doc 02 §A and §I). Modules are multi-day units, not daily lessons: a module's reading and practice land on its first day, the between-module experiment runs during the following days, the partner exchange closes the cycle. Faster (2 days/module) and stretched (4–6 days/module) variants exist; stretch every span below proportionally, but the pause beats stay full days at every cadence. They are not slack to compress out.

The course-day numbers below are calendar days from cohort start (Day 1 = content opens).

---

## Week 0 (before content opens)

| Beat | What happens | Done by | Worked example | Your dates |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Enrollment closes | Last intake submitted; every applicant screened (Doc 04 §A) | Start minus 7 days | Mon 28 Sep | |
| Pairing complete | Every learner matched; both partners signed the partner agreement (Doc 04 §B) | Start minus 3 days | Fri 2 Oct | |
| Welcome packet out | Calendar (this file, filled), referral packet, Enrollment Terms copy, feed rules pinned | Start minus 2 days | Sat 3 Oct | |
| ⚑ Pre-cohort checklist signed | Doc 04 §K, every box. **The cohort does not open with an unchecked box.** | Start minus 1 day | Sun 4 Oct | |

---

## The course span: Modules 00–11

Intensity tags: **L** Low · **M** Medium · **H** High. ⚑ marks an SLA-relevant date the CM puts in their own calendar with a reminder.

| Course days | Beat | Tag | CM marks (⚑ = SLA-relevant) | Worked example | Your dates |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1–2 | **Module 00 · Start Here & Getting Your Container** | L | ⚑ Feed monitoring begins (every post read within 48h, standing until close) · confirm every learner posted a first feed line by Day 2 | Mon 5 – Tue 6 Oct | |
| 3–5 | **Module 01 · Orientation, New Context, Radical Responsibility** | L | ⚑ Pairing tracker: first exchange logged per pair by Day 5; re-match anyone with no real exchange (Doc 02 §C.1) | Wed 7 – Fri 9 Oct | |
| 6–8 | **Module 02 · Thoughtware, Thoughtmaps, Box** | M | Partner SLA watch (24h reply windows) now routine | Sat 10 – Mon 12 Oct | |
| 9–11 | **Module 03 · Liquid State, Center-Ground-Bubble, Five Bodies** · **teach-back #1** closes the module | L | Confirm teach-back exchanges happened (logged, not heard) | Tue 13 – Thu 15 Oct | |
| 12–14 | **Module 04 · Feedback, Coaching, Rapid Learning, Experiments** | L | ⚑ Backup-CM drill run and logged before any High module (Run-Book Part 5) | Fri 16 – Sun 18 Oct | |
| 15–17 | **Module 05 · Feelings vs Emotions, Old Map, Numbness Bar** | **H** | ⚑ Unlock checklist verified per learner before access (Doc 04 §D) · ⚑ light check-in DM to every learner · clinician confirmations on file where Screen 1/5 applied | Mon 19 – Wed 21 Oct | |
| 18–21 | **Module 06 · Mixed Emotions & EHP** (the single heaviest module; give it 4 days) · **teach-back #2** closes the module | **H** | ⚑ Unlock checklist per learner · ⚑ both partners confirm 48h reachability before anyone starts · ⚑ check-in DM to every learner during the window | Thu 22 – Sun 25 Oct | |
| 22 | **⏸ Consolidation pause**: one full low-demand day. No new content. Ground, sleep, journal, the 15-minute consolidation recall rep, partner check-in. Not skippable at any cadence. | — | ⚑ Watch the feed closely today; post-EHP tenderness surfaces here, not during Module 6 | Mon 26 Oct | |
| 22 (evening) | **Mid-course integration call** (~75 min, optional attendance, after Module 6 on purpose: integration, not warm-up). No recording of shares, per Doc 04 §H. | — | Written teaching summary to absentees within 48h | Mon 26 Oct, evening | |
| 23–25 | **Module 07 · Low Drama, Gremlin, Responsible Game** | **H** | ⚑ Unlock checklist per learner · ⚑ check-in DM | Tue 27 – Thu 29 Oct | |
| 26–28 | **Module 08 · Listening, Speaking, Communication** (heavy Medium; the partner exchange *is* the module) | M | Confirm every pair scheduled the 45-minute exchange | Fri 30 Oct – Sun 1 Nov | |
| 29 | **⏸ Rest day**: the low-demand beat before the last High module. No new content. | — | Good day for the CM to clear the action log backlog | Mon 2 Nov | |
| 30–32 | **Module 09 · Ego States, Problem Ownership, Learning Spiral** · **teach-back #3** closes the module | **H** | ⚑ Unlock checklist per learner · ⚑ check-in DM · clinician confirmations where Screen 5 applied | Tue 3 – Thu 5 Nov | |
| 33–35 | **Module 10 · Map of Possibility, Bright/Shadow, Is-Glue, Three Powers** | L | Watch for learners racing the ending; offer the stretched close | Fri 6 – Sun 8 Nov | |
| 36–38 | **Module 11 · Continuation: 90-Day Container & Possibility Team** · closing partner exchange · Learning Self-Assessment re-rate + anonymous submission window opens ([Learning Self-Assessment](Learning%20Self-Assessment.md)) | L | ⚑ Closing-witness requests: any learner whose partner is unreachable gets a witness for the closing declaration (bench or CM) | Mon 9 – Wed 11 Nov | |
| 38 (evening) | **End-of-course integration call** (~90 min): declarations round, gratitude round, what's-next pointers | — | Same recording rules as mid-course | Wed 11 Nov, evening | |

---

## After the close

| Beat | What happens | When | Worked example | Your dates |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cohort close | Materials-access reminder, stopping-well notes to non-finishers, feed wind-down posted | Close + 0–3 days | by Sat 14 Nov | |
| Assessment window closes | Anonymous aggregate compiled (Learning Self-Assessment, collection flow) | Close + 7 days | Wed 18 Nov | |
| ⚑ Post-cohort review | Doc 04 §L: action-log read-through, learning aggregate, completion-by-module, one concrete improvement | Close + 7–14 days | week of 23 Nov | |
| **Alumni touchpoint · 30 days** | Short CM message to every finisher: *"30 days since close. Are your two practices still running? Anything you want a witness for?"* Points at the 30-day self check-in the learner calendared in Module 11. | Close + 30 days | Fri 11 Dec | |
| **Alumni touchpoint · 60 days** | Same shape. Add: Possibility Team status, if the cohort converted ([Possibility Team Conversion Guide](Possibility%20Team%20Conversion%20Guide.md)) | Close + 60 days | Sun 10 Jan | |
| **Alumni touchpoint · 90 days** | Same shape, plus close: what's-next pointers, witness-bench invitation to graduates, and the end of the operator's data-retention window (Enrollment Terms §7) | Close + 90 days | Tue 9 Feb | |

---

## Notes for the operator filling this in

1. **The pause beats are load-bearing.** The consolidation day after Module 6 and the rest day after Module 8 cushion the only stretches where High modules stack. Cohorts that compress them are the cohorts that lose people silently in week three.
2. **High modules do not unlock on mismatched pairings.** If partners drift more than one module apart for 4+ days, the witness-bench protocol runs (Doc 04 §I, [Witness Bench Onboarding](Witness%20Bench%20Onboarding.md)) before the calendar resumes for that learner.
3. **One calendar per learner is the ideal.** Learners on stretched cadence get their own filled copy; the cohort-level dates (calls, close, touchpoints) stay shared.
4. **Every ⚑ goes into the CM's own calendar with a reminder**, not just this document. The Run-Book's standing weekly loop (Part 2) covers everything between flags.

---

# Cohort Feed Templates

| | |
|---|---|
| **For** | Community Manager (CM) running the cohort feed of an async Expand the Box cohort |
| **When** | Open this file before the cohort opens; use it weekly through closure |
| **Reads alongside** | `02 - Async Delivery Framework.md` Section D · `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` Sections F, H |

The cohort feed is **narrow on purpose.** Not a discussion board, not a support group, not a place for theory debate or long therapeutic confessionals: those have other homes (partner exchange, referral list, separate PM communities). The feed's one job: each learner posts a small specific signal (*I am still in the container, here is one thing alive in me*) and reads other learners' signals to know the cohort is alongside them.

The CM's job is to seed prompts that produce that kind of post, redirect drift toward it, and triage what shows up. Doc 04 holds the triage rubric and platform privacy minimums. This file holds the prompts and the language.

---

## 1. Cohort feed welcome post

The CM pins this at the top of the feed the day the cohort opens, before any module prompts go up. Verbatim:

```
Welcome. This is the cohort feed.

FOR:
  - One line of what landed in the module you just finished.
  - One line of what is sticking sideways.
  - Optional: one question for the group.
  - Optional: a witness offer — "if anyone needs a third party to 
    listen to something this week, I have 20 min Thursday."

NOT FOR:
  - Long processing of personal material. That goes to your partner.
  - Other people's stories.
  - Advice to other learners. The course teaches witnessing, not 
    advising. Resist the urge.
  - PM concept debate. Different forum — ask me and I'll point you.

RULES:
  - I read every post within 48h.
  - I classify in my head: GREEN (normal, no action), YELLOW (check-
    in DM within 24h), RED (active crisis — same-day response with 
    crisis line numbers in my first message).
  - Confidentiality holds; what's in the feed stays in the cohort.
  - You can delete your own posts; you can ask me to hide a post.

If a post feels too big for the feed, it probably is. Send it to 
your partner. If too big even for your partner, send it to me or 
use the referral list. The partner agreement applies here too — 
same confidentiality, same not-advising stance, same immediate-
danger protocol.

One line is enough. Often the smaller the post, the more it lands.

— [CM name]
```

---

## 2. Per-module cohort feed prompts

The CM posts one prompt to the feed at the start of each module. One or two sentences. The prompt asks for a small specific signal, never a story. Each prompt is voice-message-ready; the CM can post it as text, audio, or both.

**Day 1: Orientation, New Context, Radical Responsibility.**

> What sentence did you choose in the red-pill ceremony? Post the sentence (not the story).

**Day 2: Thoughtware, Thoughtmaps, Box Technology.**

> Name one move your Box made this week that you caught in the act. One sentence. (Not the story of the situation — the move itself.)

**Day 3: Liquid State, Center-Ground-Bubble, Five Bodies.**

> Which of the five bodies is loudest in you today, and which is quietest? Two words: loud body / quiet body.

**Day 4: Feedback, Coaching, Rapid Learning, Experiments.**

> What is one piece of feedback you received from your partner that landed somewhere unexpected? One sentence about what it landed in (the body location or the part of life). Not what they said.

**Day 5: Feelings vs Emotions, Old Map of Feelings, Numbness Bar.**

> Name one feeling you noticed under your Numbness Bar this module. One word.

**Day 6: Mixed Emotions and Emotional Healing Process.**

> Two-feeling combination you noticed in yourself this module. Format: "[feeling] + [feeling]." No story.

**Day 7: Low Drama, Gremlin Food, Shifting to Responsible Game.**

> One Low Drama role you caught yourself playing this week — Victim, Persecutor, or Rescuer. One sentence on the move, not the situation. (And: did the gremlin enjoy it?)

**Day 8: Listening, Speaking, Communication, Completion Loops.**

> Of the four Listenings, which one was hardest to hold in your Day 8 partner exchange? One word: defensive / verbatim / listening / possibility.

**Day 9: Ego States, Problem Ownership, Learning Spiral.**

> Which ego state surfaced for you most clearly in this module's work? One word. (And: did you let it speak, or did you collapse back to a different state?)

**Day 10: Map of Possibility, Bright Principles, Three Powers, Integration.**

> One declaration you are taking forward. (Not an intention; a declaration.)

---

## 3. What posts look like (good)

Examples the CM can hold as the shape. These are not posted as templates; they are reference for the CM's eye when triaging.

> **Day 1.** "Red pill. I am in." First time I have said something to myself I knew I meant.

> **Day 5.** Anger. Located in my jaw. I had been calling it tiredness for about six years.

> **Day 7.** Rescuer. The move was over-explaining in a work meeting so a colleague would not have to think. Gremlin loved it.

> **Day 10.** "I am the author of what my mornings become." Said it out loud to the room. Then again to my partner. Holding.

These posts share three traits: short, specific, and bring the signal back to *the learner*, not to the situation, not to other people, not to a theory.

---

## 4. What posts look like (drift)

Posts that need redirect, with the CM's verbatim DM language for each. The CM redirects privately, and may hide or delete the original depending on severity.

**Drift 1: long therapeutic confessional.** *"My mother always told me… [600 words]… and last night my husband again did the thing where…"*

```
Hi [name] — what you posted is real material; it belongs in your 
partner exchange, not the feed. I'll hide the post (text preserved 
— I'll send it back privately if you want it). Take it to [partner] 
as your next exchange. If it's bigger than the pairing can hold, 
use the referral list.
```

**Drift 2: advice to another learner.** *[Reply.] "I had something similar! What worked for me was…"*

```
Hi [name] — I'm hiding your reply. Not ill-intended — the feed 
isn't a place for advice between learners. The course teaches 
witnessing, not advising. What you do with the impulse: name what 
you noticed in yourself while reading their post, and say only 
that. If you want to offer a third-party witness call, the welcome 
post has the format.
```

**Drift 3: theory debate.** *"I'm not sure I agree with the four-levels frame — in my experience the boundary between adolescent and adult is more about… [400 words]"*

```
Hi [name] — the feed isn't for theory debate. The content is fixed; 
arguing it as a cohort turns the feed into a study group, a 
different gameworld. Here is the PM community where that lives: 
[link]. I'll hide your post; repost it there if you like.

Different question: is the thing you're arguing with conceptually 
hard to sit with as practice? If yes, partner exchange.
```

**Drift 4: witness offer that's actually advice in disguise.** *"If anyone is stuck on Day 5, I have a great technique I learned in another course I'd share over a call."*

```
Hi [name] — generous instinct. But "I'll share a technique" is 
teaching, not witnessing. A witness offer is: "if you need someone 
to listen to something for 20 min this week, I have Thursday." No 
technique, no teaching, no fixing. Repost in that format?
```

---

## 5. Cohort feed triage decision log

The CM keeps a short internal log of every post and DM, classified per the triage rubric in `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` Section F. The log protects the learner and the CM. It logs metadata only (the action taken), never the content of the post. Brief is fine.

Template per row:

| Date | Learner ID | Module | Post type | Flag | CM action | Follow-up date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-02-12 | L-04 | Day 5 | Feed post | Green | None | — |
| 2026-02-12 | L-09 | Day 5 | Feed post | Yellow (stuck feeling >24h, named) | DM sent, offered voice call | 2026-02-13 |
| 2026-02-13 | L-09 | Day 5 | DM reply | Yellow (still stuck, decline call, accept stretched cadence) | Cadence adjusted, partner informed | 2026-02-15 |
| 2026-02-13 | L-11 | Day 5 | Feed post | Red (suicidal ideation, no plan named) | Crisis line numbers sent, clinician referral, cohort paused | 2026-02-14 |

The log lives in the same private spreadsheet as the pairing tracker (Doc 04 Section C), accessible only to the CM and their clinical supervisor. End-of-cohort review (Doc 04 Section L) pulls from this log.

---

## 6. Mid-course "what's alive" check-in prompt

Between Day 5 and Day 6 (after the first High module, before the most demanding one), a softer prompt that lets learners register where they are without producing material. Posted to the feed.

```
Mid-course check-in. Not a module prompt — a pause prompt.

Pick one. Post one. One sentence max.

  - The thing I came in looking for is _______ closer / further 
    away / different than I thought.
  - The body part loudest right now is _______.
  - The pace I am on is _______ right / too fast / too slow.
  - What I most want my partner to know about this week is _______.

No need to explain. I am reading.
```

The CM reads these within 24h (tighter than the normal 48h, because of mid-course intensity). Any "too fast" triggers a cadence DM. Any partner-related answer triggers a pairing-tracker check.

---

## 7. End-of-cohort gratitude prompts

After Day 10. Two posts the CM seeds.

**Partner-to-partner (learners reply tagging their partner).**

```
Pairings closed today. Cohort closes this week.

If you want to thank your partner publicly, this is the post. One 
or two sentences. Tag them. Specific is better than generic — what 
you carry forward FROM them, not "thanks for being amazing."

Thanking them in private only is also clean. The public version 
lets the cohort witness that the container held.
```

**Partner-to-cohort (CM seeds the next day, learners reply).**

```
Last post on this feed.

If you want to say one thing to the cohort — not to a person, to 
the room — say it here. The room held you while you held your 
partner while your partner held you. That circulation is the 
cohort.

One line. What you take with you. What you are leaving here.

Feed closes for posting [date, 7 days out], archived read-only for 
[duration]. Pairing continuation (if any) is between you and your 
partner; the cohort as a structure ends here.

Thank you for being in this container.

— [CM name]
```

After the gratitude window, the CM runs the post-cohort review (Doc 04 Section L).

---

The feed is small on purpose. Twelve good one-line posts per module is healthy. Three long posts per module is the feed pulling toward the wrong attractor: fix the prompts, fix the redirects, fix the welcome post if drift is persistent. Signal-to-noise on the feed is the CM's clearest read on whether the cohort is using the pairing or substituting the feed for it.

---

# Enrollment Terms and Learner Agreement

| | |
|---|---|
| **For** | The enrolling learner (read before paying) · the operator and CM (this is the instrument they hold you to, and that holds them) |
| **What it is** | The plain-language agreement for joining a held cohort of Expand the Box (Digital) |
| **Reads alongside** | `04 - Container and Gatekeeping Protocol.md` (the safety law this agreement points into) · `03 - Safety and Facilitation Framework.md` · [Referral List](Referral%20List.md) · `LICENSE.md` in the course root |

This document is written in plain language on purpose. If any sentence here is unclear, ask the operator before you pay. An agreement you did not understand is not an agreement.

---

## 1. What you are buying, and what you are not

**The content of this course is free. Forever. For everyone.**

Every module, map, practice script, and tool in Expand the Box (Digital) is licensed CC BY-SA 4.0, consistent with the spirit of World Copyleft. You can read all of it, copy it, share it, and run the solo track without paying anyone anything. That is not a loophole. It is the design. The honest free path is the solo track described in Module 0.

**What you purchase is the held container.** Specifically:

1. **Screening.** A real intake process (the five screens in Doc 04 §A) run by a qualified human, so that everyone in your cohort, including you, has been checked for fit before the work starts.
2. **A community manager (CM).** A named, qualified person (the bar is Doc 04 §J) who monitors the cohort, triages what surfaces, answers you within the response windows in section 4 below, and routes anyone who needs more than this course toward the right support.
3. **Partner pairing.** A matched practice partner before Module 1, the pairing tracker, and re-matching if the pairing does not work.
4. **The cohort feed.** A private, moderated space with other people doing the same work in the same weeks.
5. **The witness bench.** Backup witnesses so that an absent partner does not strand you before a High-intensity module.
6. **The cohort calendar.** Live integration calls, scheduled teach-backs, the pause beats, and the 30/60/90-day alumni check-ins after the course closes.

Read the list again. Nothing in it is content. If you leave the cohort, you keep every page of the course. What ends is the container.

---

## 2. Who can enroll

**You must be 18 or older.** This course works with emotional and psychological material in an unsupervised, asynchronous format. It is built for adults and the intake form asks you to confirm your age. There is no under-18 version of this course.

You must also complete the intake screening honestly (section 3). Some answers route to a conversation with the CM rather than to automatic enrollment. Routing is not rejection. It is the container doing its job.

---

## 3. Your commitments (what you sign up to do)

By enrolling you agree to the following. These are the same obligations the course documents place on every cohort member; they are gathered here so you can see them in one place before you pay.

1. **Answer the intake honestly.** The five screening questions (suicidality, trauma, addiction, coercive environment, mania/psychosis) exist to keep you and your cohort safe. An intake answered falsely defeats the screening you are paying for, for yourself and for the people paired with you. If your circumstances change mid-course, you tell the CM.
2. **Sign and keep the partner agreement.** Before any exchange, you and your partner both acknowledge the 7-line partner agreement (Doc 04 §B): reply within 24 hours, witness rather than advise, hold confidence, act on the immediate-danger question, tell the CM if you cannot continue, never reuse what your partner shares, and no sexual, financial, or professional relationship with your partner during the course. A learner who will not sign item 4 of that agreement (the immediate-danger protocol) cannot be paired and therefore cannot join a cohort.
3. **Keep feed conduct.** The cohort feed is for short, specific reflections about your own work. No advice to other learners, no long confessionals (those belong in the partner exchange), no other people's stories, no theory debate. The CM redirects drift privately; repeated redirection is a conversation, not a strike system.
4. **Use the safety equipment as instructed.** The grounding script, the pause practices, the consent checks before High-intensity modules, and the unlock checklist are part of the course, not optional extras. The container can only hold someone who is using it.
5. **Pace honestly.** You choose your cadence (Doc 02 §I). You do not skip the pause beats, and you do not unlock High modules by clicking through the checklist untruthfully.

---

## 4. Our commitments (what the operator and CM sign up to do)

1. **Response windows.** Routine messages to the CM are answered within 24 hours. Feed posts are read within 48 hours. Anything the triage rubric flags yellow gets a direct message within 24 hours. Anything flagged red gets an immediate response, with a first reply within 15 minutes of the CM or their named backup seeing it, and crisis-line numbers in that first message (Doc 04 §F–G).
2. **CM coverage.** A qualified CM (Doc 04 §J) holds your cohort for its full span, with a named, briefed backup covering any window when the CM is unreachable. If CM coverage fails and cannot be restored, the cohort pauses and section 6 applies.
3. **Privacy.**
   - **No analytics.** The course pages contain no tracking, no analytics scripts, no third-party calls.
   - **Your practice data stays in your browser.** The interactive tools store everything locally on your device. Nothing you type into a practice tool is transmitted anywhere. You can export or delete it at any time.
   - **The feed is closed.** Visible to your cohort only, never public, posts deletable by their author.
   - **The CM logs metadata only.** The pairing tracker records that exchanges happened, never what was said. Personal shares on live calls are never recorded.
   - **Assessment is anonymous.** The Learning Self-Assessment is collected only as a no-name aggregate ([Learning Self-Assessment](Learning%20Self-Assessment.md)).
   - The full platform privacy minimums are in Doc 04 §H, and every component of the platform stack must meet them before your cohort opens.
4. **Screening is real.** Every learner in your cohort passed the same intake you did. Nobody is waved through.
5. **Honesty about scope.** This course is not therapy, coaching, or psychiatric care, and the operator will never present it as a substitute for any of those. You receive a referral packet for your region at enrollment, before content access opens.

---

## 5. Refunds (the whole tree, plainly)

"Cohort start" means the day content access opens for your cohort. "Platform costs" means the actual per-seat costs the operator already spent on you (payment processing fees and per-seat platform fees), which the operator states as a fixed amount on the enrollment page before you pay.

| When you withdraw | What you get back |
|---|---|
| **Before cohort start** | Everything. Full refund, no questions, no form. |
| **During week one** (cohort start through the day before Module 5 unlocks for you) | Everything minus platform costs. One short exit message to the CM is requested (not required) so your partner can be re-matched. |
| **After Module 5 unlocks for you** | Prorated: the share of the course still ahead of you, counted by modules not yet unlocked out of twelve, minus platform costs. Example: you leave with 4 of 12 modules still locked, you get one third back, minus platform costs. |
| **Safety-routed exit, at any point** | **Everything. Always. Full refund.** No deductions, no proration, regardless of timing. |

A **safety-routed exit** is any ending that the safety framework initiated rather than you simply changing your mind: an intake screen routed you away from this cohort, a triage escalation paused your access and you and your clinician chose not to resume, or the operator ended your participation under section 6. The course does not keep money it took from someone the container could not hold. There is no scenario in which leaving for safety reasons costs you anything.

In every refund case you keep all course materials. They were free before you paid and they stay free after.

---

## 6. Termination for safety (routing, not punishment)

The operator can pause or end your participation in the cohort if the safety framework requires it: a red-flag escalation under Doc 04 §G, material that exceeds what this container can hold, or conduct that makes the container unsafe for other learners (for example, repeatedly breaking the partner agreement's confidentiality line).

Three things are always true when this happens:

1. **It is routing, not punishment.** The message you receive will name the next right container for you (clinician, crisis support, a later cohort, the solo track) and your referral packet stands. Pause and exit under this section are framed as care because they are care.
2. **You get a full refund** (section 5, safety-routed exit).
3. **You keep the materials and the license.** Ending the container never ends your access to the work.

A paused learner resumes when the learner and their support person agree it is appropriate (Doc 04 §G step 4). An ended enrollment can re-apply for a future cohort; the re-application path is stated in the exit message.

---

## 7. The fine print, still in plain language

- **Not therapy.** This course is education and practice. The CM is not your clinician. Nothing here diagnoses or treats any condition.
- **Your data.** The operator holds your intake answers and contact details for the cohort span plus the alumni touchpoint window (90 days after close), then deletes them on request or per the stated retention policy on the enrollment page. Screening answers are never shared with other learners, including your partner.
- **The license.** All course content is CC BY-SA 4.0. Full terms in `LICENSE.md`. This agreement governs the container only; it places no restriction on the content.
- **Changes.** If the operator changes these terms, the version you signed governs your cohort.
- **This is an independent re-presentation of Possibility Management thoughtware, not an official Possibility Management training.** That honesty line is in every footer of this course and it belongs here too.

---

## 8. Acknowledgment

Signed at intake, alongside the screening acknowledgments in Doc 04 §A.3:

- [ ] I am 18 or older.
- [ ] I have read these terms and the refund tree, and I understand what I am buying is the held container, not the content.
- [ ] I answered the intake screening honestly and will tell the CM if my circumstances change.
- [ ] I will sign the partner agreement before exchanges begin, including the immediate-danger protocol.
- [ ] I understand this course is not therapy, coaching, or psychiatric care, and I have received the referral packet for my region.
- [ ] I understand that a safety pause or exit is routing, not punishment, and always comes with a full refund.

Name: ______________________ Date: ______________
